items.title: CEDEAO : le lancement de la monnaie unique ouest-africaine confirmé pour 2027
items.description: a l'issue de leur 69ᵉ sommet ordinaire, tenu le 19 juillet a Freetown, en Sierra Leone, les chefs d'etat et de gouvernement de la Communaute economique des etats de l'Afrique de l'Ouest (CEDEAO) ont reaffirme leur engagement a lancer la monnaie unique, l'ECO, en 2027. L'introduction de la devise se fera cependant par etapes. Les premiers pays admis devront remplir les criteres de convergence macroeconomique definis par la Communaute et disposer des institutions necessaires au fonctionnement de la future union monetaire. Les autres etats beneficieront d'un accompagnement afin de rejoindre ulterieurement le dispositif. La CEDEAO presente l'ECO comme un instrument destine a approfondir l'integration economique regionale, a faciliter les echanges et a soutenir une croissance durable, resiliente et inclusive. Pour integrer la premiere phase, les etats devront notamment maintenir leur deficit budgetaire sous le seuil de 3 % du produit interieur brut (PIB), contenir l'inflation annuelle sous les 10 % et disposer de reserves en devises couvrant au moins trois mois d'importations. La Sierra Leone, le Liberia, le Nigeria, le Ghana, la Guinee et la Gambie sont cites parmi les pays susceptibles de former le premier groupe. Leur participation restera toutefois conditionnee au respect des criteres de convergence et a l'achevement des mecanismes de gouvernance de la monnaie commune. Des obstacles encore nombreux Lors de leur precedent sommet a Abuja, les dirigeants ouest-africains avaient exprime leur inquietude face aux resultats insuffisants des etats en matiere de convergence macroeconomique et aux retards accumules dans l'execution de la feuille de route. Depuis, des consultations ont ete engagees entre la Commission de la CEDEAO, les gouverneurs des banques centrales et l'Agence monetaire de l'Afrique de l'Ouest afin de parvenir a un consensus sur les questions techniques encore en suspens. Les chefs d'etat ont egalement salue l'enregistrement de la marque « ECO » aupres de l'Organisation africaine de la propriete intellectuelle. Une avancee institutionnelle et symbolique, alors que les fondations economiques de la future devise restent encore a consolider. La Guinee a par ailleurs ete autorisee a rejoindre la Task Force presidentielle chargee du programme. Cette instance doit se reunir avant le prochain sommet ordinaire de la CEDEAO, prevu en decembre 2026. Le FRAPP denonce un projet sans fondations Le Front pour une revolution anti-imperialiste, populaire et panafricaine (FRAPP), mouvement citoyen base a Dakar et militant pour une sortie du franc CFA, a critique les decisions annoncees a l'issue du sommet de Freetown. Il estime que la CEDEAO doit « cesser de vendre des illusions aux peuples », invoquant les reports successifs du lancement de la monnaie unique depuis plus de vingt ans. Pour le mouvement, « le probleme n'est donc plus le calendrier, mais l'orientation meme du projet », a rapporte la presse senegalaise le 21 juillet. Se referant au rapport 2024 de l'Agence monetaire de l'Afrique de l'Ouest (AMAO), en l'absence de donnees consolidees pour 2025, le FRAPP affirme que « seul le Cap-Vert devrait satisfaire a l'ensemble des criteres de premier rang » sur la periode 2024-2026. Selon le mouvement, « lancer l'ECO en 2027 releve davantage de la communication politique que d'une perspective serieuse ». Il estime egalement qu'« une monnaie unique ne peut pas remplacer une volonte politique commune » et appelle les etats ouest-africains a privilegier leur souverainete monetaire, tout en developpant des mecanismes regionaux de solidarite et de cooperation monetaire.

items.title: WAFCON 2026: Cameroon target redemption as Indomitable Lionesses chase continental glory
items.description: After missing the previous edition, Cameroon return to the TotalEnergies CAF Women’s Africa Cup of Nations Morocco 2026 determined to re-establish themselves among Africa’s elite. The Indomitable Lionesses, one of the continent’s most successful women’s national teams, secured a place at the expanded 16-team tournament after receiving a second chance through CAF’s revised qualification system. Now, under newly appointed head coach Valentine Nguele and inspired by emerging midfield star Naomi Eto, Cameroon will be aiming not only to reclaim their place among Africa’s leading nations but also to secure qualification for the 2027 FIFA Women’s World Cup. Nickname: Indomitable Lionesses Appearances: 13 Last appearance: 2022 Best finish: Runners-up (2004, 2014, 2016) FIFA Ranking: 71st (June 2026) CAF Ranking: 6th (June 2026) Head Coach: Valentine Nguele Road to the tournament Cameroon’s route to Morocco 2026 was anything but straightforward. The Indomitable Lionesses were eliminated in the final qualifying round after suffering a 3-1 aggregate defeat to Algeria, seemingly ending their hopes of returning to Africa’s biggest women’s football competition. However, CAF’s decision to expand the tournament from 12 to 16 teams opened the door for four additional nations to qualify based on their FIFA rankings. Ranked among Africa’s highest-placed teams outside the automatic qualifiers, Cameroon earned one of those places and were handed a lifeline to return to the continental finals. The Indomitable Lionesses had entered the qualification campaign in the second round after receiving a bye in the opening stage, but now have another opportunity to prove they remain one of African football’s traditional powerhouses. The coach: Valentine Nguele Cameroon head into the tournament under new leadership following the appointment of Valentine Nguele on 13 June. Nguele earned widespread recognition through her outstanding work with FC Ebolowa Girls, transforming the club into one of the dominant forces in Cameroon’s Guinness Super League. Her tactical discipline, meticulous preparation and ability to build resilient teams helped guide the club through an impressive 16-match unbeaten run, establishing her reputation as one of the country’s leading coaches. Known for combining demanding standards with strong relationships with her players, Nguele now faces the biggest challenge of her coaching career. Her appointment also reflects Cameroon’s confidence in home-grown coaching expertise as the federation looks to usher in a new era for the national team. Player to watch Naomi Eto (Midfielder) At just 21 years old, Naomi Eto has rapidly emerged as one of the brightest talents in African women’s football. The Paris Saint-Germain midfielder, currently gaining valuable experience on loan at Italian side Sassuolo, arrives in Morocco carrying the hopes of a new generation of Cameroonian footballers. Named Cameroon’s Women’s Player of the Year in 2025, Eto is admired for her intelligence on the ball, creativity, vision and ability to dictate the rhythm of matches from midfield. Before making the move to Europe, she developed into one of the standout performers in the domestic league with FAP, where her consistent displays marked her out as one of the country’s most complete players. She is expected to play a central role in Cameroon’s ambitions throughout the tournament. Cameroon at the Women’s AFCON Cameroon boast one of the richest histories in the Women’s Africa Cup of Nations. The Indomitable Lionesses have reached the final on three occasions—in 2004, 2014 and 2016—but are still searching for their first continental title. Their consistency has also been reflected in three third-place finishes in 2002, 2012 and 2018, giving Cameroon six podium finishes overall. Only Nigeria and South Africa have accumulated more top-three finishes in the history of the competition, underlining Cameroon’s long-standing status among Africa’s leading women’s football nations. Although the trophy has so far eluded them, the Lionesses have regularly challenged the continent’s strongest teams and remain one of the most respected sides in African football. Did you know? With six podium finishes, Cameroon are the third-most successful nation in Women’s AFCON history behind Nigeria and South Africa. TotalEnergies CAF Women’s AFCON 2026 outlook After the disappointment of missing the 2024 tournament, Morocco 2026 offers Cameroon an opportunity to begin a new chapter. With a respected new coach, an exciting generation led by Naomi Eto and a proud tradition of competing at the highest level, the Indomitable Lionesses will believe they have the quality to challenge for honours once again. Beyond ending their long wait for a first Women’s AFCON title, Cameroon will also be targeting one of the four automatic qualification places for the 2027 FIFA Women’s World Cup in Brazil. For one of Africa’s traditional giants, the mission is clear: restore the pride of the Indomitable Lionesses and prove that Cameroon remain a force capable of competing with the very best on the continent.

items.title: NSS investigating GH¢60 allowance deductions as refund decision awaits probe outcome
items.description: Deputy Director of the National Service Authority, Lieutenant Colonel Moses Kpeungu, says the investigation is ongoing following complaints from some national service personnel over deductions from their meagre monthly allowances. The National Service Secretariat says it is investigating deductions from the allowances of national service personnel under the Capacity Building Programme, adding that a decision on refunds will be made after the probe is concluded. Speaking on JoyNews Desk on Wednesday, July 22, the Deputy Director of the NSS said between 50,000 and 52,000 service personnel were covered under the arrangement, although he could not provide an exact figure. Responding to concerns about when affected personnel would receive refunds, he said the authority is taking the matter seriously and had already put measures in place to establish the facts. “The authority is not sitting aloof. We have put in measures to ensure that the right things are done. We are going to investigate the issue, identify those who were involved and those whose monies were deducted,” he said. According to him, any decision to refund the deductions will depend on the investigation’s findings. “If the committee comes out with its findings and the monies have to be paid, why not?” he stated. The Deputy Director, however, noted that the review would also consider administrative costs associated with the Capacity Building Programme. “The issue is about those who participated in the capacity-building exercise. Some administrative costs might have gone into it. The authorities, in consultation with the government, will take a decision,” he added.

items.title: Inflation : hausse de l'IPC de 0,3% en juin
items.description: L'indice des prix à la consommation (IPC) a enregistré, en juin dernier, une hausse de 0,3% comparativement au même mois de l'année 2025, selon le Haut-Commissariat au Plan (HCP). Cette évolution est la conséquence de la hausse de l’indice des produits non alimentaires de 2,3% et de la baisse de celui des produits alimentaires de 2,3%, explique le HCP dans une note d'information relative à l'IPC de juin 2026. Pour les produits non alimentaires, les variations vont d’une baisse de 0,3% pour la «Communication» à une hausse de 6,7% pour le «Transport». Comparé au mois de mai dernier, l'IPC a connu, en juin 2026, une baisse de 0,4% par rapport au mois précédent. Cette variation est le résultat du repli de 0,8% de l’indice des produits alimentaires et de 0,2% de l’indice des produits non alimentaires. Les baisses des prix des produits alimentaires observées entre mai et juin 2026 concernent principalement les «Légumes» avec 5,2%, le «Lait, fromage et œufs» (1,9%), les «Viandes» et le «Pain et céréales» (0,3%) et les «Huiles et graisses» (0,1%). En revanche, les prix ont augmenté de 3,4% pour les «Poissons et fruits de mer», de 0,8% pour les «Fruits» et de 0,2% pour le «Café, thé et cacao». Pour les produits non alimentaires, la baisse a concerné principalement les prix des «Carburants» avec 2,9%. Les baisses les plus importantes de l’IPC ont été enregistrées à Beni-Mellal avec 2%, à Kénitra (1,7%), à Errachidia (1,4%), à Al-Hoceima (1,3%), à Tétouan (1%), à Fès (0,8%), à Laâyoune (0,6%), à Settat (0,4%) et à Agadir, Casablanca, Rabat, Meknès et Dakhla (0,3%). En revanche, des hausses ont été enregistrées à Safi avec 0,4% et à Oujda avec 0,1%. Dans ces conditions, l’indicateur d’inflation sous-jacente, qui exclut les produits à prix volatiles et les produits à tarifs publics, aurait connu au cours du mois de juin 2026 une hausse de 0,2% par rapport au mois de mai 2026 et une baisse de 0,1% comparativement à juin 2025. The post Inflation : hausse de l'IPC de 0,3% en juin appeared first on Hespress Français - Actualités du Maroc .

items.title: Osun Election: SDP appeals judgment recognising governorship candidate
items.description: …Seeks stay of High Court judgement purporting to overrule Supreme Court judgement on leadership By Henry Umoru ABUJA- THE National Leadership of the Social Democratic Party, SDP urged the Court of Appeal in Abuja to stay the execution of a Federal High Court judgment recognising Olaitan Salawu-Adeniyi as its governorship candidate for the 2026 Osun State governorship election, pending the determination of its appeal. The SDP has also asked the appellate court to restrain the Independent National Electoral Commission from implementing the June 24, 2026 judgment delivered by Justice M.G. Umar of the Federal High Court, Abuja, until the appeal is determined. In a statement on Wednesday by its National Publicity Secretary, Araba Rufus Aiyenigba, SDP noted that court documents showed that the appeal, marked CA/ABJ/CV/1011/2026, arose from the judgment in Suit No. FHC/ABJ/CS/402/2026. According to the party, the Federal High Court judgment directed INEC to enforce an earlier Court of Appeal judgment delivered on March 27, 2026, recognising Shehu Gabam as the party’s national chairman, despite the party’s position that the Supreme Court had set aside that decision on May 22, 2026. “The Federal High Court judgment ordered INEC to enforce a Court of Appeal judgment which, according to the party, had already been set aside by the Supreme Court. The party believes the High Court’s decision is inconsistent with the Supreme Court’s ruling.” The statement read,”the Federal High Court Abuja judgement delivered on 24th June 2026 ordered INEC to enforce a Court of Appeal judgement in FAYEMI TOSIN BABATUNDE VS INEC delivered on 27th March 2026 to install Shehu Gabam as National Chairman but the Supreme Court has set aside the judgement on 22nd May 2026 whereupon INEC complied and reinstated Prof Sadiq Umar Abubakar Gombe as National Chairman. ” It is judicial rascality and legal nullity for a High Court to contradict the Supreme Court or purport to revive a judgement which the Supreme Court has voided and set aside. “SDP seeks an order of the Court of Appeal to stay execution of the void order of the Federal High Court delivered by Justice MG Umar while the Appellate Court hears the appeal by the Social Democratic Party. It also seeks an order restraining the Independent National Electoral Commission from giving effect to the illegal and null order of the Federal High Court. “The suit arose over Osun State Governorship primary between the plaintiff Salawu Olaitan and expelled former National Chairman Shehu Gabam and one Ogbonna Okechukwu former National Youth Leader all of whom the SDP denies as non members of the party. “The Social Democratic Party (SDP) has approached the Court of Appeal, Abuja Judicial Division, seeking an order staying the execution of a Federal High Court judgment that recognised Salawu-Adeniyi Mustapha Olaitan as the party’s governorship candidate for the 2026 Osun governorship election, pending the determination of its appeal. “The application, contained in a Motion on Notice filed before the appellate court, also seeks to restrain the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) from taking any steps to implement the judgment until the appeal is heard and determined. “Court documents showed that the appeal, marked CA/ABJ/CV/1011/2026, arose from the judgment delivered on June 24, 2026, by Justice M.G. Umar of the Federal High Court, Abuja, in Suit No. FHC/ABJ/CS/402/2026. “The SDP is the appellant/applicant in the matter, while Salawu-Adeniyi Mustapha Olaitan, INEC, the party’s expelled National Chairman, Alhaji Shehu Musa Gabam, and expelled National Youth Leader Uchechukwu Chukwuma, who are both facing criminal prosecution at the Wuse Magistrate court are also listed as respondents. “The motion was brought pursuant to Section 17 of the Court of Appeal Act, Order 4 Rule 6(2) of the Court of Appeal Rules, 2021, and the inherent jurisdiction of the court. “Among the principal reliefs sought is an order for stay of the execution of the Federal High Court judgment pending the hearing and determination of the appeal. “The party is also asking the appellate court to restrain INEC, either by itself, its officers, agents or privies, from enforcing or giving effect to the judgment pending the determination of the appeal. “The dispute centres on the Federal High Court’s orders relating to the recognition of Olaitan Salawu as the SDP’s governorship candidate for the Osun governorship election. “In its application, the SDP argued that the appeal raises substantial and arguable issues requiring the consideration of the Court of Appeal. “The party contended that unless the execution of the judgment is suspended, the subject matter of the appeal could be altered before the appellate court has an opportunity to make pronouncement on the issues in dispute. “It maintained that implementation of the judgment, particularly by INEC, could render the appeal nugatory if it eventually succeeds. “According to the application, the interest of justice would be better served by preserving the existing state of affairs pending the determination of the substantive appeal. “The party further urged the Court of Appeal to exercise its discretionary powers to prevent a situation where the appeal would become merely academic after irreversible steps had been taken in compliance with the Federal High Court’s decision. “The application forms part of the continuing legal battle over the SDP’s leadership and the nomination of its candidate for the Osun governorship election. “The Court of Appeal has yet to fix a date for the hearing of the motion or rule on the reliefs sought by the SDP.” The post Osun Election: SDP appeals judgment recognising governorship candidate appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Séno: Le CRGE alerte sur la persistance des maladies épidémiques et des décès maternels
items.description: Burkina-Liptako-Santé-Épidémies-Surveillance Séno: Le CRGE alerte sur la persistance des maladies épidémiques et des décès maternels Dori, 21 juil. 2026 (AIB) – La région du Liptako/Soum a enregistré, à la 28e semaine de 2026, 5 001 cas graves de paludisme, 2 032 cas de rougeole, 881 cas suspects de dengue, 26 cas de méningite et 84 décès maternels, selon les données présentées lundi à Dori lors de la première session annuelle du Comité régional de gestion des épidémies (CRGE), présidée par le gouverneur de la région, Abdoul Karim Zongo. Le rapport présenté par le directeur régional de la Santé, Djibrilla Alou Maiga, montre que le paludisme reste la première cause de morbidité et de mortalité dans la région. À la 28e semaine de l'année, 5 001 cas graves ont été enregistrés, dont 30 décès, soit un taux de létalité de 0,6 %. Ces cas représentent 4,8 % des 103 072 cas recensés au niveau national. Comparativement à la même période de 2025, la situation s'est toutefois améliorée avec une baisse de 2 256 cas et de 45 décès, la région ayant alors enregistré 7 257 cas graves et 75 décès. La rougeole demeure également préoccupante avec 2 032 cas notifiés et 21 décès, représentant 17,6 % des 11 489 cas enregistrés au Burkina Faso. Le taux de létalité régional est estimé à 1 %. La dengue a, pour sa part, totalisé 881 cas suspects sans décès, contre 1 323 cas et deux décès à la même période en 2025, soit une diminution de 442 cas. Les autorités sanitaires ont insisté sur la nécessité de renforcer la surveillance épidémiologique, l'investigation rapide des foyers de maladies et la capacité de prise en charge des patients. Le comité a également examiné la situation des décès maternels. Au total, 84 décès ont été enregistrés dans la région, dont 67 dans le district sanitaire de Dori, 12 à Gorom-Gorom, deux à Djibo, deux à Sebba et un en communauté. Ces décès représentent 13,6 % des 618 décès maternels enregistrés au Burkina Faso. En neuf ans, la région a totalisé 1 039 décès maternels, avec une moyenne de 115 décès par an et un pic de 150 cas en 2024. Le Liptako/Soum occupe ainsi le troisième rang national, derrière les régions du Kadiogo (132 cas) et du Guiriko (81 cas). Au Centre hospitalier régional de Dori, 65 décès maternels ont été enregistrés. Plus de la moitié des patientes provenaient du district sanitaire de Dori (34), suivies des districts de Tougouri (14), Gorom-Gorom (9), Manni (5) et Bogandé (3). Face à cette situation, le gouverneur Abdoul Karim Zongo a salué l'appui de la Croix-Rouge, qui a contribué à l'organisation de cette session, avant d'inviter les services techniques, les organisations de la société civile, les ONG et les partenaires techniques et financiers à renforcer leurs actions afin d'améliorer les indicateurs sanitaires. Le directeur régional de la Santé a, pour sa part, exhorté les populations à assainir davantage leur cadre de vie et à participer activement à la campagne de chimioprévention du paludisme saisonnier (CPS), dont le lancement est imminent. Selon lui, cette mobilisation est indispensable en cette période hivernale marquée par une recrudescence des cas de paludisme et de dengue. Si certains indicateurs, notamment ceux du paludisme et de la dengue, sont en baisse par rapport à l'année précédente, les autorités administratives et sanitaires demeurent préoccupées par l'ampleur des décès maternels et de la rougeole. Elles appellent l'ensemble des acteurs et des communautés à une mobilisation accrue pour prévenir les épidémies et renforcer la protection de la santé des populations. Agence d'information du Burkina AMM/bak

items.title: Rejecting cedi coins is a criminal offence punishable by law – BoG warns traders
items.description: The Bank of Ghana (BoG) has cautioned traders, transport operators, and businesses that refusing to accept Ghana cedi coins as payment for goods and services is a criminal offence punishable by law. In a public notice, the central bank said it had observed the “widespread and persistent refusal” by some individuals and businesses to accept 1, 5, 10, 20, and 50 pesewa coins, as well as GH¢1 and GH¢2 coins. According to the BoG, all coins issued by the Bank remain legal tender and must be accepted for the settlement of debts and transactions nationwide. It stressed that no trader, transport operator, business entity, or individual has the discretion to reject the coins on grounds of inconvenience, low value, or personal preference. The bank explained that under the Currency Act, 1964 (Act 242), refusing to sell goods or services because a customer is paying with legal-tender coins or notes constitutes an offence unless the currency has been demonetised or withdrawn from circulation. It noted that offenders, upon conviction, could face imprisonment of up to three years, a fine, or both. The BoG further warned that anyone who encourages or instructs others to reject coins, including business owners who direct their staff to refuse them, commits the same offence and is liable to the same penalties. It added that persons caught committing the offence may be arrested without a warrant. The central bank urged the public to stop rejecting cedi coins, warning that persistent offenders risk arrest, prosecution, fines, or imprisonment. It said it would collaborate with the Ghana Police Service and other law enforcement agencies to ensure compliance with the law. The BoG also encouraged members of the public to report incidents of coin rejection to the nearest Bank of Ghana office, the Ghana Police Service, or through the bank’s official communication channels. The bank called on all individuals, businesses, and institutions to accept and handle Ghana’s currency in all its denominations responsibly and in accordance with the law.

items.title: Trump approves 30-year nuclear pact with Saudi Arabia
items.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has approved a landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia, which would potentially pave the way for uranium enrichment on Saudi soil. According to U.S. media reports, the Wall Street Journal, citing government officials, the deal would help the kingdom build a civilian nuclear programme. The multibillion-dollar agreement is said to have a duration of 30 years, secure a key role for U.S. companies in building Saudi Arabia’s nuclear infrastructure and exclude other foreign competitors. A central provision calls for U.S. companies to build a uranium enrichment facility in Saudi Arabia, provided a joint U.S.-Saudi study concludes that such a step is justified. The Trump administration reportedly considers that this partnership would give the U.S. influence over the Saudi nuclear programme while ensuring it would not be misused for military purposes. The agreement, which is to be submitted to Congress in Washington for review in the coming days, is expected to face resistance, as many lawmakers oppose the spread of nuclear technology in the politically volatile Middle East. The New York Times reported, citing government officials, that Trump had been forced to make compromises to reach the agreement. He had agreed to conditions that could potentially prevent international inspectors from gaining access to certain sites, specifically those where there is suspicion that nuclear fuel could be diverted for weapons programmes, the newspaper said. U.S. Energy Secretary Chris Wright already held talks on such a deal during a visit to the region in April 2025. At the time, speculation was already swirling about a possible new energy agreement between the two countries that could pave the way for a nuclear programme in the desert kingdom. The U.S. previously held talks with Saudi Arabia on a possible normalisation of bilateral relations with Israel. In return, support for Riyadh on its nuclear programme and a possible new security agreement had been discussed. However, the Saudi-Israeli normalisation process was frozen after the outbreak of the Gaza war at the end of 2023. Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, the kingdom’s de facto ruler, has continued to express his country’s “great interest” in developing a civilian nuclear programme. In a 2018 interview with CBS, he said Saudi Arabia would pursue nuclear weapons “as soon as possible” if Iran acquired them. Saudi Arabia and Iran have long competed for regional influence. Although the two countries restored diplomatic ties in 2023 under a Chinese-brokered agreement, deep mistrust remains after decades of geopolitical rivalry, much of it shaped by the Sunni-Shia divide. The post Trump approves 30-year nuclear pact with Saudi Arabia appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: PM Shehbaz hails Pakistan's 'remarkable progress' in indigenous autonomous capabilities in defence, security fields
items.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif on Wednesday said that Pakistan has made “remarkable progress” in developing indigenous defence capabilities. He made the remarks at the Indus Robotics and Autonomous Systems (RAS) Expo 2026 being held today and tomorrow at the Pak-China Friendship Centre in Islamabad. Addressing the event, PM Shehbaz noted that Pakistan has “made remarkable progress in developing indigenous autonomous capabilities in the field of defence and security under the dynamic leadership” of Chief of Defence Forces (CDF) and Chief of the Army Staff Field Marshal Syed Asim Munir. “Our armed forces have adopted cutting-edge technologies, some of which I just witnessed on display outside this hall. Remarkable achievement,” the premier said. He added that he also took a look at simulators which were “100 per cent manufactured and assembled in Pakistan”. He noted that drones had achieved 60pc indigenisation and “many other important machines” have been made in Pakistan. The prime minister recalled that the “valiant armed forces resolutely guarded our physical and cyber frontiers through the highly effective use of advanced autonomous systems” led by the CDF and supported by the air and naval chiefs. More to follow

items.title: Feux de forêt en Algérie : ce que montrent les images satellites
items.description: De nombreuses régions d'Algérie sont en proie aux flammes depuis plusieurs jours. Les wilayas du Nord-Est sont particulièrement touchées, comme l'indiquent les chiffres de la Protection civile, confirmés par les images satellites qui montrent l'étendue de la catastrophe. Depuis le 8 juillet, 1.555 incendies ont été enregistrés à travers le pays, a indiqué la Protection civile mardi 21 juillet. Le même jour, 170 feux se sont déclarés dans 19 wilayas de l'est de l'Algérie, selon la même source. Le ministère de l’Intérieur a livré un premier bilan des pertes humaines, faisant état du décès de 6 personnes depuis le début de la vague d'incendies à travers le pays. La journée de mardi a été cauchemardesque notamment dans les wilayas de Skikda, Annaba, Guelma et El Tarf. A Annaba, les forêts des hauteurs de Seraidi ont pris feu et les flammes se sont dangereusement rapprochées des zones habitées, endommageant 150 habitations. Des habitants ainsi que les patients d'un hôpital ont dû être évacués dans l’urgence. « Une véritable catastrophe a été évitée », selon les mots du ministre de l’Intérieur Saïd Sayoud qui s’est déplacé à Annaba dans la nuit de mardi à mercredi, avec d’autres ministres. Incendies en Algérie : les images satellites montrent l’étendue de la catastrophe Le ministre de l'Intérieur Saïd Sayoud a annoncé la “poursuite des enquêtes sécuritaires pour déterminer les causes de ces incendies”. Les feux qui ravagent l’est algérien sont d'une telle ampleur qu’ils sont visibles depuis l'espace. Des images du satellite européen Sentinel 2 ont été partagées par Al Jazeera. Les images, analysées par l'unité “Open Source” du média qatari, montrent d'épaisses colonnes de fumée s'élevant de plusieurs foyers dispersés sur une vaste étendue couvrant tout le nord-est de l'Algérie, allant des environs de Skikda à l'ouest jusqu'à Annaba et El Tarf à l'est, en passant par les zones montagneuses de l’intérieur et autour du lac Fetzara, au sud d’Annaba. Ce mercredi matin, la Protection civile a actualisé son bilan, faisant état de 195 incendies en 24 heures, dont 142 éteints et 53 toujours actifs dans les wilayas de Skikda (18 feux), El Tarf (7), Tlemcen (5), Béjaïa (4) Annaba et Guelma (3). L’article Feux de forêt en Algérie : ce que montrent les images satellites est apparu en premier sur TSA .

items.title: Wontumi brought to Accra High Court for Tano Nimiri case while serving 20-year sentence
items.description: The jailed Ashanti Regional Chairman of the New Patriotic Party (NPP), Bernard Antwi-Boasiako , popularly known as Chairman Wontumi , has made his first appearance at the Accra High Court in connection with the Tano Nimiri illegal mining case . Mr Antwi-Boasiako arrived at the court in a prison bus and was escorted by prison officers after spending two nights in custody following his 20-year prison sentence in the separate Samreboi illegal mining case . The embattled politician, dressed in a neatly tucked white long-sleeved shirt and black trousers, was ushered into the courtroom through a rear entrance in an apparent move to avoid public attention. His appearance before the court comes while he is serving a custodial sentence imposed after he was convicted over illegal mining activities linked to the Samreboi concession in the Western Region. The Tano Nimiri case forms part of separate legal proceedings involving allegations related to illegal mining activities, with Mr Antwi-Boasiako expected to face the court as the matter progresses. The development comes amid heightened public interest following his recent conviction, which has triggered political reactions and legal debate, with the NPP indicating its intention to pursue available legal avenues. The court is expected to continue with proceedings in the Tano Nimiri case as the parties present their respective arguments.

items.title: Nayala: l’initiative « Toma ville propre » lancée pour faire de l’assainissement un engagement citoyen durable
items.description: Burkina-Nayala-Curage-Caniveaux Nayala: l'initiative « Toma ville propre » lancée pour faire de l'assainissement un engagement citoyen durable Toma, le 18 juil. 2026 (AIB) – Le haut-commissaire de la province du Nayala, Honoré Frédéric Paré, a procédé samedi au lancement officiel de l'initiative « Toma ville propre » à travers une vaste opération de curage des caniveaux. Cette activité citoyenne, appelée à se poursuivre chaque samedi, ambitionne de renforcer l'assainissement, de prévenir les maladies liées à l'insalubrité et de promouvoir la responsabilité collective dans toute la province. Le lancement de l'initiative « Toma ville propre » s'est déroulé devant le calvaire de Toma en présence des autorités administratives et communales, des représentants des villages ainsi que la mobilisation des populations. Le haut-commissaire de la province du Nayala, le camarade Honoré Frédéric Paré, a salué le civisme et le sens de responsabilité des participants. Il a souligné que cette initiative sera étendue à toute la province et a exhorté les chefs de famille, les commerçants et l'ensemble des citoyens à contribuer, soit par leur travail, soit par un appui matériel ou financier. Le président de la Délégation spéciale de Toma, Zakaria Sawadogo, s'est réjoui de l’adhésion des populations. Il a indiqué que cette opération se poursuivra désormais tous les samedis afin d'inscrire durablement la salubrité dans les habitudes des citoyens. Selon lui, l'initiative ne se limite pas au curage des caniveaux, mais englobe également le nettoyage des quartiers et des espaces publics. Il a rappelé que les riverains ont la responsabilité d'entretenir les caniveaux situés devant leurs concessions et leurs commerces afin de prévenir les inondations et leurs conséquences. Le président de la CPAVC/Nayala, Yaya Paré, a invité l'ensemble des villages de la province à organiser régulièrement des opérations de nettoyage des lieux publics pendant l'hivernage. Il a insisté sur le lien entre la propreté, la prévention des maladies et le bien-être des populations, tout en appelant chacun à éviter de déverser les ordures dans les caniveaux. Le haut-commissaire a donné le coup d'envoi de l'opération en descendant lui-même dans un caniveau pour effectuer les premiers coups de pelle. Les travaux se sont poursuivis jusqu'à la mi-journée dans une ambiance de forte mobilisation citoyenne. Les participants se sont donné rendez-vous pour le samedi suivant, avec l'ambition de faire de « Toma ville propre » un mouvement durable au service d'un cadre de vie sain. Agence d'information du Burkina KTA/bak

items.title: Lawyers need practical knowledge of maritime trade to resolve disputes effectively — Igbokwe
items.description: By Godwin Oritse ABUJA — President of the Nigerian Maritime Law Association (NMLA), Mr. Mike Igbokwe (SAN), has urged legal practitioners to deepen their understanding of maritime trade and commercial operations to effectively handle disputes arising in the sector. Speaking at the ongoing International Maritime Seminar for Judges in Abuja, Igbokwe said the rapidly evolving global maritime industry requires lawyers to keep pace with emerging regulations, changing business practices, technological advancements, environmental obligations and increasingly complex risk profiles. According to him, developments in the maritime sector now affect every aspect of the industry, including shipping, ports, terminals, cargo, finance, insurance, regulation and dispute resolution, creating both new responsibilities and opportunities for stakeholders. “Across the world, maritime trade is being reshaped by new regulations, shifting commercial practices, disruptive technologies, environmental obligations and increasingly complex risk profiles,” he said. “These developments affect ships, ports, terminals, cargo, finance, insurance, regulation, dispute resolution and the daily decisions of maritime businesses. They create new responsibilities, but they also open new opportunities for shipowners, terminal operators, financiers, insurers, regulators, ports, cargo interests and the lawyers who advise them.” Igbokwe said the changing dynamics of the industry make it imperative for lawyers and other stakeholders to move beyond theoretical knowledge and acquire a practical understanding of maritime transactions. He noted that with continuing supply chain disruptions, growing concerns over port efficiency, maritime security and trade facilitation, as well as emerging opportunities in the blue economy, legal practitioners must understand how contracts, insurance, trade finance, port operations, regulatory compliance and dispute resolution function in practice. According to him, such knowledge is essential to supporting national economic growth, boosting investor confidence and enhancing Nigeria’s competitiveness in regional and global trade. “When we look across generations—from the wisdom of the past, through the demands of the present, and into the possibilities of the future—we must acknowledge one truth: the rules of the maritime game are changing,” he said. “If we do not understand those changes, respond to them and help shape them, we risk being left behind. Our duty is therefore clear. We must draw strength from the experience of those who came before us, confront today’s realities with competence and courage, and prepare intelligently for the future of maritime law practice and the maritime industry.” The Senior Advocate of Nigeria also described maritime transport as the lifeline of global trade, stressing that an efficient legal framework and speedy resolution of maritime disputes are critical factors in attracting foreign investment. He noted that prospective maritime investors assess the strength of a country’s legal and regulatory framework, as well as the ability of its courts to resolve commercial disputes promptly before committing investments. According to Igbokwe, the timely determination of maritime cases is particularly important because such disputes often involve vessels, cargoes and other assets worth substantial sums of money. The post Lawyers need practical knowledge of maritime trade to resolve disputes effectively — Igbokwe appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Burkina: trois corps sans vie repêchés en une journée, dans les Hauts-Bassins
items.description: La Brigade nationale de sapeurs-pompiers (BNSP) a annoncé, ce mercredi 22 juillet 2026 sur sa page Facebook, que la Cinquième Compagnie d'Incendie et de Secours de Banfora est intervenue à deux reprises, le mardi 21 juillet, pour repêcher des corps sans vie d'une femme enceinte et de deux enfants dans les Hauts-Bassins. Face à ces drames, elle exhorte les populations à redoubler de prudence à proximité des points d'eau. La Cinquième Compagnie d'Incendie et de Secours de Banfora a été mobilisée à deux reprises, le mardi 21 juillet 2026, pour des opérations de repêchage de corps sans vie, selon une information rendue publique, ce mercredi 22 juillet 2026, par la Brigade nationale de sapeurs-pompiers (BNSP) sur sa page Facebook. La première intervention s'est déroulée dans le village de Sipigui, situé dans la commune de Orodara, à environ 45 kilomètres de Banfora. Les secours y ont repêché le corps sans vie d'une femme enceinte tombée dans un puits. Quelques heures plus tard, les sapeurs-pompiers sont intervenus dans le village de Sarékadougou, à une vingtaine de kilomètres de Banfora, où ils ont procédé au repêchage des corps sans vie de deux enfants retrouvés dans une étendue d'eau. À la suite de ces interventions, la Brigade nationale de sapeurs-pompiers appelle les populations à observer la plus grande prudence aux abords des puits, des plans d'eau et des zones humides considérées comme dangereuses. Elle recommande également de sécuriser les puits à l'aide de dispositifs de protection adaptés afin de réduire les risques de noyade et d'accidents. Ces deux drames rappellent l'importance des mesures de prévention, particulièrement en cette période où les points d'eau et les zones humides présentent davantage de dangers. La BNSP insiste sur la responsabilité de chacun pour renforcer la sécurité autour de ces espaces et éviter de nouvelles pertes en vies humaines. Par Valentin SOMANDE L’article Burkina: trois corps sans vie repêchés en une journée, dans les Hauts-Bassins est apparu en premier sur Wakat Séra .

items.title: Un nuevo modelo de OpenAI provoca un ataque “sin precedentes” contra otra plataforma de inteligencia artificial
items.description: La compañía OpenAI ha anunciado que uno de sus modelos aún no publicado, en colaboración con ChatGPT Sol 5.6, habían provocado un ataque “sin precedentes” contra HuggingFace, una plataforma de modelos abiertos de IA. Los empleados de la compañía dieron una misión compleja en un entorno aislado a sus modelos. Pero, por su cuenta, los modelos lograron salir el entorno cerrado y atacaron HuggingFace porque creyeron que era el lugar más probable donde podrían encontrar una solución. Seguir leyendo

items.description: Passoré : 𝐋𝐚 𝐒𝐎𝐏𝐀𝐌𝐈𝐁 𝐁𝐨𝐮𝐛𝐨𝐮𝐥𝐨𝐮 𝐢𝐧𝐯𝐞𝐬𝐭𝐢𝐭 𝐩𝐥𝐮𝐬 𝐝𝐞 𝟐𝟎𝟎 𝐦𝐢𝐥𝐥𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐬 𝐅𝐂𝐅𝐀 𝐩𝐨𝐮𝐫 𝐚𝐦é𝐥𝐢𝐨𝐫𝐞𝐫 𝐥𝐚 𝐦𝐨𝐛𝐢𝐥𝐢𝐭é 𝐝𝐞𝐬 𝐜𝐨𝐦𝐦𝐮𝐧𝐚𝐮𝐭é𝐬 𝐫𝐢𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐚𝐢𝐧𝐞𝐬 Yako, 20 juil. 2026(AIB)-La SOPAMIB Bouboulou SA, filiale de la SOPAMIB, a offert le 17 juillet 2026, deux infrastructures routières totalisant 5,8 kilomètres aux populations riveraines de la mine d’or de Bouboulou. Il s’agit de deux axes routiers en terre battue d’une largeur de piste de 7 m au profit des populations de dix localités impactées par les activités de la mine, dont Bouboulou, Pelegtenga, Koala, Rawèma, Sabou, Ouedkiengho, Ragounda, Taonsgho, Gonsin et Sassa. Le premier axe relie Pelegtenga à Sabou sur une distance de 3,6 Km et le second axe quant à lui relie Pelegtenga à la mine de Bouboulou sur une distance de 2,2 Km. Les sections inondables de ces deux axes routiers sont dotées d’ouvrages de franchissements afin de rendre ces routes praticables même en saison de pluie. La cérémonie de remise officielle de ces voiries intervenue le 17 juillet 2026 à Pelegtenga, a réuni autorités administratives, coutumières et religieuses de la Province du Passoré ainsi que les populations des localités bénéficiaires. D’un coût global de 𝐩𝐥𝐮𝐬 𝐝𝐞 𝟐𝟎𝟎 𝐦𝐢𝐥𝐥𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐬 𝐝𝐞 𝐟𝐫𝐚𝐧𝐜𝐬 𝐂𝐅𝐀, cette réalisation intervient avant même l’entrée en production de la mine. Selon le Directeur Général de SOPAMIB Bouboulou SA, Yannick Clovis SANOU, elle s’inscrit dans un vaste programme de développement au profit des communautés riveraines et constitue la première phase du développement des infrastructures routières dans les villages impactés par ce projet industriel. Au nom des communautés bénéficiaires, le chef de Canton de Sassa, Naaba Ligdi, a salué la réalisation de ces infrastructures routières, qu'il considère comme une avancée majeure pour améliorer la mobilité des populations et faciliter les déplacements. Tout en reconnaissant que les 5,8 kilomètres de routes constituent une première étape, il a exprimé le souhait de voir d'autres infrastructures routières être réalisées à l'avenir afin de renforcer davantage le désenclavement de la localité. Il a également formulé des vœux pour le bon déroulement des travaux de construction de la mine. Tout en saluant la contribution des autorités coutumières, religieuses et des populations à la réussite du projet, le Président de la Délégation spéciale (PDS) de la commune de Yako, Boukaré ZOMODO, a également souligné que ces infrastructures routières contribueront à améliorer la mobilité des populations, l'accès aux services sociaux de base, la circulation des biens et le dynamisme économique de la commune. Il a enfin exprimé la reconnaissance de la commune envers la SOPAMIB Bouboulou SA pour ce partenariat fructueux. Présidant la cérémonie, le Haut-commissaire de la province du Passoré, Daouda SANGARE, a salué l’engagement de la société en déclarant qu'« 𝑒𝑛 𝑠𝑎 𝑞𝑢𝑎𝑙𝑖𝑡é 𝑑𝑒 𝑠𝑜𝑐𝑖é𝑡é 𝑑’E𝑡𝑎𝑡, 𝑙𝑎 𝑆𝑂𝑃𝐴𝑀𝐼𝐵 𝐵𝑜𝑢𝑏𝑜𝑢𝑙𝑜𝑢 𝑐𝑜𝑛𝑐𝑖𝑙𝑖𝑒 𝑑𝑒 𝑚𝑎𝑛𝑖e𝑟𝑒 𝑒𝑥𝑒𝑚𝑝𝑙𝑎𝑖𝑟𝑒, 𝑠𝑎 𝑚𝑖𝑠𝑠𝑖𝑜𝑛 𝑑𝑒 𝑐𝑟é𝑒𝑟 𝑑𝑒 𝑙𝑎 𝑟𝑖𝑐ℎ𝑒𝑠𝑠𝑒 𝑝𝑜𝑢𝑟 𝑙𝑎 𝑁𝑎𝑡𝑖𝑜𝑛, 𝑒𝑡 𝑠𝑜𝑛 𝑑𝑒𝑣𝑜𝑖𝑟 𝑑'𝑖𝑚𝑝𝑎𝑐𝑡𝑒𝑟 𝑑𝑖𝑟𝑒𝑐𝑡𝑒𝑚𝑒𝑛𝑡 𝑒𝑡 𝑝𝑜𝑠𝑖𝑡𝑖𝑣𝑒𝑚𝑒𝑛𝑡 𝑙𝑒 𝑞𝑢𝑜𝑡𝑖𝑑𝑖𝑒𝑛 𝑑𝑒𝑠 𝑐𝑜𝑚𝑚𝑢𝑛𝑎𝑢𝑡é𝑠 𝑟𝑖𝑣𝑒𝑟𝑎𝑖𝑛𝑒𝑠. 𝐸𝑛 ℎ𝑜𝑛𝑜𝑟𝑎𝑛𝑡 𝑠𝑒𝑠 𝑒𝑛𝑔𝑎𝑔𝑒𝑚𝑒𝑛𝑡𝑠 𝑎𝑣𝑎𝑛𝑡 𝑚e𝑚𝑒 𝑑’𝑒𝑛𝑡𝑟𝑒𝑟 𝑒𝑛 𝑝ℎ𝑎𝑠𝑒 𝑑𝑒 𝑝𝑟𝑜𝑑𝑢𝑐𝑡𝑖𝑜𝑛, 𝑙𝑎 𝑆𝑂𝑃𝐴𝑀𝐼𝐵 𝐵𝑜𝑢𝑏𝑜𝑢𝑙𝑜𝑢 𝑝𝑜𝑠𝑒 𝑙𝑒𝑠 𝑏𝑎𝑠𝑒𝑠 𝑑'𝑢𝑛 𝑝𝑎𝑟𝑡𝑒𝑛𝑎𝑟𝑖𝑎𝑡 𝑠𝑎𝑖𝑛, 𝑡𝑟𝑎𝑛𝑠𝑝𝑎𝑟𝑒𝑛𝑡 𝑒𝑡 𝑑𝑢𝑟𝑎𝑏𝑙𝑒.» Grâce à l'appui de la mine d'or de Bouboulou en équipements et à la valorisation des matériaux locaux, les communautés riveraines prennent une part active à la réalisation du projet. Elles fabriquent elles-mêmes les Briques en Terre Comprimée (BTC) destinées aux infrastructures de la mine ainsi qu'aux logements de relocalisation. Sur la plateforme de production installée sur le site minier, l'Association locale Bayir Metba assure la fabrication de plus d'un million de BTC. Pour mener cette activité, elle emploie déjà environ une vingtaine de jeunes issus des villages impactés. Deux nouvelles lignes de production seront prochainement mises en service, permettant à l'association de renforcer ses capacités de production et de créer de nouvelles opportunités d’emploi au profit des communautés locales. À travers cette initiative, SOPAMIB Bouboulou SA concrétise sa vision d'un projet minier véritablement endogène, où les ressources locales, le savoir-faire des communautés et l’emploi constituent les premiers bénéfices du développement de la mine. Agence d’information du Burkina

items.title: Court adjourns trial of 5 men charged with concealing Timipre Sylva’s whereabouts
items.description: The Federal High Court in Abuja on Wednesday adjourned the trial of five men accused of failure to disclose the whereabouts of former Gov. Timipre Sylva of Bayelsa until Oct. 5. The case, which was fixed for commencement of trial before Justice Peter Lifu, could not go on because the court did not sit. The matter was subsequently fixed for Oct. 5 after the court vacation. The News Agency of Nigeria (NAN) reports that the five suspects, Reuben Ayuba, Musa Mohammed, Friday Paul, Paganengigha Anagaha and Ayebaifie Suobite, were arraigned on July 1 by the Department of State Services (DSS). They were arraigned on a two-count charge. Sylva, who is at large, and six others are currently facing a trial in a sister court presided over by Justice Joyce Abdulmalik over an alleged plan to topple President Bola Tinubu’s government. He was alleged to have a financial link to the six suspected coup plotters presently on trial. The suspects are retired Maj.-Gen. Mohammed Ibrahim Gana, retired Capt. (NN) Erasmus Ochegobia Victor, Insp. Ahmed Ibrahim, Zekeri Umoru, Bukar Kashim Goni and Abdulkadir Sani, listed as 1st to 6th defendants, respectively. The Federal Government filed a 13-count charge, marked FHC/ABJ/CR/206/2026, against the defendants. Although Sylva, who is also a former minister of state for petroleum resources in the late ex-President Muhammadu Buhari government, is not named as a defendant in the charge, he is listed as being at large. Meanwhile, after the five suspects were arraigned before Justice Lifu on July 1, they pleaded not guilty to the two-count charge marked FHC/ABJ/CR/299/2026. The Prosecution Counsel, E.G. Orubor, sought a trial date to enable them to establish their case against the defendants. Justice Lifu thereafter admitted them to N5 million bail each with two sureties each who must be residents of Abuja and provide their tax clearance certificates. This was after the defendant’s lawyers, including Sunusi Musa, SAN, moved applications for their bail. The judge adjourned the matter until July 22 for commencement of trial. In count one, the defendants were alleged to have, on or about April 28 in Abuja, become “accessories after the fact of felony by concealing the whereabouts of Timiprey Sylva, who is a fugitive of the law.” The offence is contrary to Section 519 of the Criminal Code Act LEN, 2004. In count two, they were alleged to have conspired to commit a felony by concealing the whereabouts of Sylva, who is a fugitive of the law. The offence is contrary to Section 516 of the Criminal Code Act LFN, 2004. (NAN) The post Court adjourns trial of 5 men charged with concealing Timipre Sylva’s whereabouts appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Sandbondtenga/Education: Six écoles de la CEB de Boussouma clôturent leur année scolaire 2025-2026
items.description: Sandbondtenga/Education: Six écoles de la CEB de Boussouma clôturent leur année scolaire 2025-2026 Boussouma, 18 juil. 2026 (AIB) – six écoles primaires de la Circonscription d'éducation de base (CEB) de Boussouma/S2 ont clôturé, samedi dans le village d’Antoa, leurs activités pédagogiques de l'année scolaire 2025-2026. Au cours de la cérémonie les meilleurs les élèves, les enseignants et les autres acteurs de l'éducation en présence des autorités administratives, éducatives, coutumières et religieuses. Les écoles des villages d'Antoa, de Fatin, de Fatin-Bendogo, de Pana, de Raboudoumbaoma et de Wendatenga, confrontées à des réalités similaires, ont décidé depuis 2022, d'organiser de façon tournante la clôturé de l’année scolaire afin de renforcer la solidarité entre leurs communautés. Placée sous le thème « L'école et son milieu : une synergie d'action pour une éducation résiliente et sûre », cette troisième édition, a mobilisé les différents acteurs de l'éducation et les populations locales dans une ambiance festive. La cérémonie a été marquée par des prestations culturelles des élèves, notamment des danses tradi-modernes, des chants, des récitals et des représentations théâtrales. Les meilleurs élèves, des enseignants ainsi que plusieurs acteurs ayant contribué au bon fonctionnement des établissements ont été récompensés ou distingués par des attestations de reconnaissance. Les participants ont également marqué leur adhésion à la politique de lutte contre la désertification, par une séance de plantation d'arbres dans l'enceinte de l'école hôte. Le chef de la Circonscription d'éducation de base de Boussouma/S2, Hamado Sawadogo, a salué une initiative qui, selon lui, traduit la solidarité et la fraternité entre les communautés des six villages. Il les a encouragées à poursuivre cette dynamique, estimant qu'elle constitue un modèle susceptible d'inspirer d'autres localités au service de la cohésion sociale et de la promotion de l’éducation. Il a, en outre, invité les populations à s'impliquer davantage dans la gestion des établissements scolaires afin d'accompagner les efforts de l'État pour l'amélioration de la qualité de l'éducation. Le parrain de l'édition, El Hadj Salfo Sawadogo, opérateur économique a, pour sa part, réaffirmé son engagement à soutenir les actions en faveur de l'éducation dans la localité, afin de consolider l'entente, la solidarité et le développement du système éducatif. Initiée en 2022, cette célébration tournante n'avait pas pu se tenir en 2024 et 2025 en raison de la dégradation de la situation sécuritaire. La reprise de cette activité est perçue par les populations comme un signe du retour de la paix et de la relance de la vie communautaire. Agence d'information du Burkina OW/aio/bak

items.title: El incendio de Guadalajara entra en fase de “estabilización” tras arrasar más de 32.000 hectáreas
items.description: El incendio de La Mierla (Guadalajara), el más grande de la historia de Castilla-La Mancha , que ha arrasado más de 32.000 hectáreas, ya ha entrado en fase de “estabilización”, según ha anunciado este miércoles el presidente de Castilla-La Mancha, Emiliano García-Page, desde el puesto de mando avanzado de Tamajón, en una declaración conjunta con el presidente del Gobierno, Pedro Sánchez, que ha visitado la zona afectada siete días después de que se iniciase el fuego. Antes de su declaración ante los medios, ambos han saludado a todos los miembros del dispositivo de emergencias, que llevan desde el pasado jueves trabajando en las labores de extinción. Desde allí, Sánchez ha vuelto a insistir en la “necesidad” de crear un pacto de Estado por la emergencia climática, como ya hizo en el incendio de Los Gallardos, en Almería. Seguir leyendo

items.title: FG cuts textbook assessment fees by 25% ahead of national ranking system
items.description: By Joseph Erunke, Abuja The Federal Government has reduced the fees for textbook assessment and ranking ahead of the rollout of the National Textbook Ranking System in September 2026. The move, announced by the Nigerian Educational Research and Development Council (NERDC) on Wednesday, is aimed at easing the financial burden on publishers and authors while encouraging wider participation in the new textbook evaluation process. In a statement signed by the Executive Secretary of NERDC, Prof. Salisu Shehu, the Council said the assessment fee has been reduced by 25 per cent, from N2,000 to N1,500 per page, while the ranking fee has been cut from N1 million to N750,000 per textbook title. According to the statement, publishers and authors seeking assessment and ranking of their textbooks should begin making payments based on the revised rates with immediate effect. NERDC also announced that publishers who had already paid the previous assessment fee of N2,000 per page would be refunded the excess amount, adding that details of the refund process would be communicated in due course. “The revised fees take immediate effect, and all stakeholders in the book industry are expected to comply accordingly,” the Council stated. The fee reduction comes as the Federal Government intensifies preparations for the implementation of the National Textbook Ranking System, one of the key education reforms introduced by the Minister of Education, Dr. Maruf Tunji Alausa, to improve the quality of teaching and learning materials in Nigerian schools. Under the new policy, which takes effect in September 2026, only textbooks that have been assessed, approved and nationally ranked by NERDC will be authorised for use in schools across the country. Textbooks that fail to obtain a national ranking will no longer be approved for classroom use. According to education authorities, the initiative is designed to curb the proliferation of substandard and duplicated textbooks while ensuring that students and teachers have access to high-quality instructional materials that align with the national curriculum. NERDC explained that although it will continue to perform its statutory role of approving textbooks, approval alone will no longer qualify a book for classroom use under the new framework. Instead, every approved textbook will undergo a comprehensive national evaluation by expert panels, which will assess factors including content quality, curriculum alignment, pedagogical value, accuracy and overall suitability before assigning a ranking. The Council said the ranking system will serve as a reliable guide for schools, teachers, parents and education administrators in selecting the most appropriate textbooks for different subjects and levels of education. It added that the reduction in assessment and ranking fees forms part of efforts to ensure a smooth transition to the new nationwide textbook quality assurance regime while encouraging greater participation by publishers and authors. The post FG cuts textbook assessment fees by 25% ahead of national ranking system appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: UCL 2026/27: Terry Yegbe scores in first leg qualifier as Lech Poznan beat AGF
items.description: Ghanaian defender Terry Yegbe was on the scoresheet for Lech Poznan in their UEFA Champions League qualifier against AGF on Tuesday evening. The centre-back was named in the starting lineup for the first-leg tie of the preliminary round just days after leading the club to Polish Super Cup victory. The Polish side enjoyed a great start to the game and opened the scoring after 29 minutes through Mikael Ishak. Eight minutes later, the Danish side were reduced to ten players after Jesper Hansen was sent off for a professional handball. Patrik Walemark doubled the lead for Lech before the break. The home side reduced the deficit ten minutes into the second half, but Yegbe was on hand to restore his side’s two-goal lead. Terry Yegbe scored a stunning goal in Lech Poznań’s 4-1 win over AGF in the UEFA Champions League qualifiers. #JoySports pic.twitter.com/YPtQcoLgtn — #JoySports (@JoySportsGH) July 22, 2026 The Ghanaian defender surged forward before firing a stunner which beat the AGF goalkeeper to make it 3-1 for Lech. Walemark wrapped up the win with his second of the game in the 65th minute as Lech claimed a healthy first-leg advantage. The game was only Yegbe’s second official match since signing for the club during the summer transfer window from Ligue 1 side FC Metz.

items.title: How missing Kiamaiko trader Zizo was abducted by armed men in police uniform
The DSA Isn’t Wrong About Everything
items.description: The Republican Party and its allied media organs have been blasting out messaging highlighting the radical views of the Democratic Socialists of America, whose views conservatives wish to associate with the Democratic Party. The particular extreme DSA stance they have chosen to highlight over the past week is the abolition of the U.S. Senate. The right-wing media is aflame with headlines such as “DSA Co-Chairman Defends Plan to Abolish Senate: ‘We Don’t Think That’s Extreme’” and “‘It’s Not Extreme’: Guess What the DSA Wants to Abolish Now.” (Answer: the Senate.) The odd thing about this choice is that, although the DSA’s platform is indeed chock-full of maniacal proposals, on this particular idea, the maniacs have a point. The Senate is a vestigial deformity upon the Constitution whose elimination, unlikely though it might be, would rank among the great democratic advances in American history. The Senate’s many admirers, chief among them senators themselves, tend to defend it as the product of ingenious design. Ben Sasse, a former Republican senator from Nebraska, offered up a typical tribute in a Wall Street Journal column on Sunday. He wrote, “The upper house is built for the long view, with six-year terms that encourage a deliberative trustee model of representation. Senators owe their states both judgment and conscience.” The truth is that James Madison and his allies did not want to create a Senate that represented states rather than people. The Constitution’s architects, despite notoriously excluding many Americans from the political community, wished those who were granted the right of self-determination to be able to do so on an equal basis. As Alexander Hamilton wrote in “Federalist No. 22,” “Every idea of proportion and every rule of fair representation conspire to condemn a principle, which gives to Rhode Island an equal weight in the scale of power with Massachusetts, or Connecticut, or New York.” The idea that people who lived in small states should enjoy more power than people who lived in large states offended Madison and Hamilton’s sense of justice. [ James Kirchick: The DSA is a parasite ] However, those Founders lived in the real world, and in that world, they had to cut deals with less-remembered Founders such as Gunning Bedford Jr. of Delaware, who threatened to walk away from the newly founded republic unless the citizens of small states were granted disproportionate rights. Madison and Hamilton recognized they had no choice but to submit. This deal is known to history as the Great Compromise. It was great not because it was perfect, but because it was politically necessary. As Madison explained, “A government founded on principles more consonant to the wishes of the larger States, is not likely to be obtained from the smaller States. The only option, then, for the former, lies between the proposed government and a government still more objectionable. Under this alternative, the advice of prudence must be to embrace the lesser evil.” This is an elegant way of saying: It stinks, but what are you gonna do? Merely because the Founders had to cut a deal to achieve an imperfect outcome does not bind the citizenry to abide by it for eternity. Other compromises the Founders made—such as protecting slavery—have long since fallen by the wayside. But the Senate, rather than viewed by posterity as the necessary evil that Madison recognized it to be, has been sacralized. In some ways, the Senate has evolved in a more democratic direction. Prior to the passage of the Seventeenth Amendment in 1913, senators were appointed by state legislatures, in many cases with the help of hefty bribes, rather than elected. In other ways, however, the body has grown even more undemocratic. When the Constitution was ratified, the most populous state had fewer than 13 times as many residents as the least populous state. Today, that ratio has ballooned to nearly 67 to 1. This malapportionment occurred only in part because of the natural growth of mega-states such as California, Texas, and New York. It was also the product of deliberate manipulation. In the late 19th century, Republicans, seeking to maintain their electoral advantage in the chamber, admitted a series of states that would not have met the customary qualifications for statehood. Most comically, they split the lightly settled Dakota territory into two states, giving them an extra pair of senators. [ Eric W. Orts: The path to give California 12 senators, and Vermont just one ] The upper chamber’s undemocratic character has been warped further by the filibuster. The Constitution specified only limited uses (such as approving treaties and proposing constitutional amendments) for a supermajority, but it has evolved into a routine requirement for legislation. In theory, a Senate bloc representing less than 11 percent of the public could prevent the passage of a law. This imbalance has been made all the more noxious by yet another distortion. Small states (many of them located in the sparsely populated Great Plains and Mountain West) tend to be rural and overwhelmingly white, which means that the Senate systematically under-weights racial minorities, who tend to live in states with the lowest per-capita voting clout. One 2025 paper calculated that, compared with a legislative body that represents all racial groups equally, in the U.S. Senate, white people functionally enjoy 1.14 votes per person while Black people have 0.84 votes, Asian Americans 0.75, and Latinos 0.67. This form of affirmative action for white people draws little notice but has an important effect. Studies have found that the Senate has a persistent tilt in favor of Republican candidates and conservative-policy outcomes . The tilt may be worse than it appears, given that Republicans have often squandered their structural advantage by nominating extreme candidates who have lost winnable races. The DSA’s platform sets out an ambition to reduce the Senate to a symbolic institution like the House of Lords, which once exerted counter-majoritarian influence over the United Kingdom but now simply affirms policies enacted by the democratically elected Parliament. That is obviously an ambitious goal with no prospect of fulfillment anytime soon. Even the more modest goal of admitting Washington, D.C., and Puerto Rico as states, which would reduce but not eliminate the Senate’s conservative tilt, is probably not in reach for the next Democratic majority. But advocating for morally just, utopian policies is the sort of thing that democratic socialists ought to be doing. Republicans want to scare voters into thinking that if Democrats win the midterm elections, the United States will be on the road to a future in which the Senate is no more—or is, at best, an impotent debate club for elderly statesmen. If only!

items.title: Última hora de la actualidad política, en directo | Feijóo: “Sin el Gobierno de Sánchez, Zapatero no habría podido delinquir”
items.description: El líder del PP, Alberto Núñez Feijóo, ha vuelto a cargar contra el Gobierno por los casos de corrupción que acechan el entorno del presidente Pedro Sánchez. “Hay corrupción institucional: condena al fiscal general del Estado. Hay corrupción política: condena al ministro Ábalos. Y hay corrupción en el entorno familiar: condena al hermano del presidente del Gobierno”, ha enumerado durante su balance del curso político. Feijóo se ha referido al caso del expresidente socialista José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero. “Se pasó años dando lecciones de moralismo y de paz y resulta que todo era una cuestión de negocios”, ha dicho. “Lo más lamentable es que, sin el Gobierno de Sánchez, Zapatero no habría podido delinquir”. Este miércoles, la presidenta del PSOE, Cristina Narbona, comparece en la comisión de investigación del Senado sobre el caso Koldo. Está citada por el PP para explicar su relación con Leire Díaz, exmilitante socialista investigada por urdir presuntamente una trama para boicotear causas judiciales que pudieran afectar al Gobierno. Además, la Audiencia Nacional retoma con los informes finales de las defensas, entre ellas la del exministro del Interior Jorge Fernández Díaz, el juicio del caso Kitchen , la presunta operación parapolicial para sustraer documentación al extesorero del PP Luis Bárcenas. Seguir leyendo

items.title: Photos: Peter Obi meets Swiss Ambassador, seeks stronger partnership on Poverty Reduction others
items.description: The presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC), Mr. Peter Obi, says stronger international partnerships are essential to advancing Nigeria’s development, particularly in education, healthcare and poverty reduction. Obi disclosed this in a post on his verified X handle on Wednesday following a meeting with the Swiss Ambassador to Nigeria, Mr. Patrick Egloff. According to him, the meeting focused on Nigeria’s development journey, with discussions centred on key sectors critical to improving the lives of citizens. “I met with the Swiss Ambassador to Nigeria, Mr. Patrick Egloff, and we had a productive and engaging discussion on our country’s developmental journey, with particular emphasis on the critical sectors that drive development: education, healthcare, and lifting people out of poverty,” Obi said. He expressed appreciation to the Swiss Confederation for its continued support to Nigeria, particularly in the areas of economic development, humanitarian assistance, cultural cooperation and peace-building efforts in the North-East. Obi said deepening collaboration with development partners based on shared values and a commitment to human development would help build a more prosperous and inclusive nation. “Building stronger partnerships founded on shared values and a commitment to human development remains essential to creating a more prosperous and inclusive Nigeria,” he stated. He concluded the post with his signature message: “A New Nigeria is Possible.” The post Photos: Peter Obi meets Swiss Ambassador, seeks stronger partnership on Poverty Reduction others appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Kourittenga/Commune de Dialgaye : Une brigade territoriale de gendarmerie installée pour renforcer la présence sécuritaire
items.description: BURKINA-KOURITTENGA-INSTALLATION-COMPAGNIE-GENDARMERIE NATIONALE Kourittenga/Commune de Dialgaye : Une brigade territoriale de gendarmerie installée pour renforcer la présence sécuritaire Dialgaye, 21 juil. 2026 (AIB)- Le Président de la délégation spéciale (PDS) de la commune de Dialgaye, Nogwaya Joseph Sawadogo, a présidé, ce mardi 21 juillet 2026, l'installation de la brigade territoriale de la Gendarmerie nationale de Dialgaye. Cette unité prend le relais de la Police nationale, présente dans la commune depuis 1985. La cérémonie a réuni les autorités administratives, coutumières, religieuses, militaires et paramilitaires, ainsi qu'une forte mobilisation de la population. Implanté depuis 1985 dans la commune de Dialgaye, le commissariat de Police de Dialgaye couvrait la commune qui compte 30 villages, en plus de la commune Tensobentenga dans la province du Kourittenga. Conformément au décret portant détermination des compétences territoriales de la Police nationale et de la Gendarmerie nationale, ainsi qu’à la circulaire relative à la mise en œuvre de la deuxième phase du plan de déploiement des services des deux forces, la Brigade de Police nationale de Dialgaye, opérationnelle depuis 1985, a cédé sa place, ce mardi 21 juillet 2026, à une brigade territoriale de Gendarmerie nationale. Le commissaire, Robert T Yameogo a exprimé sa profonde gratitude aux plus hautes autorités et a, au nom du personnel du commissariat de Police, traduit sa reconnaissance aux autorités locales et à la population pour la franche collaboration. Le commandant de la brigade de gendarmerie de Dialgaye, Issoufou Ouédraogo, a exprimé sa reconnaissance à la hiérarchie pour sa nomination en qualité de commandant à la tête de cette Brigade de gendarmerie à Dialgaye. « Je voulais du fond du cœur remercier le commissaire sortant et son équipe pour leur disponibilité et pour tout ce qu’ils ont fait pour la commune de Dialgaye », a-t-il exprimé. Le Samand-Naaba, représentant du chef de Dialgaye, a salué l’ensemble des efforts consentis par le commissaire et son équipe et a souhaité la bienvenue au commandant de la gendarmerie et ses éléments. Le Président de la délégation spéciale (PDS) de la commune de Dialgaye, Nogwaya Joseph Sawadogo, a rassuré la disponibilité de l’administration au commandant de la gendarmerie et son équipe tout en souhaitant bon vent au commissaire et ses éléments pour la suite de leur mission. Agence d’information du Burkina AWS/HB/OO

items.title: Reform military budgeting process to reduce corruption risks – Dr Sowatey
items.description: Criminologist and security analyst Dr Emmanuel Sowatey has called for reforms to Ghana’s military budgeting process, stronger parliamentary oversight and improved public understanding of the security sector to address corruption risks in the country’s defence sector. Speaking on Joy FM’s Super Morning Show during the Corruption Watch segment on Wednesday, July 22, Dr Sowatey outlined what he described as four key recommendations for government following discussions on a report that rated Ghana’s defence sector as being at high risk of corruption. Responding to a question from the host on what advice he would give the government, Dr Sowate said the issues go beyond the findings of the report. “I’ll go outside the report to show that, one, the problem is systemic. It’s referring to the issue of how to best have value for money in military expenditure. It’s not only the expenditure. It’s also the budgeting process.” He said the priority should be making military budgeting more consistent with the country’s democratic values while striking the right balance between national security and public accountability. “The first thing I would say is that let’s make the military budgeting process more reflective of our democratic governance. The line between national security and transparency can be something very tricky, but it can always be dealt with.” His second recommendation focused on improving what he described as “security sector literacy” among the public, journalists and other stakeholders. According to him, a better understanding of how the security sector operates would enable citizens to ask informed questions about the use of public resources and whether defence spending aligns with national security objectives. “We need security sector literacy, because that will then let you ask the right questions about how resources are being used, and how those resources further the spirit and the letter of the defence policy… and then the security policy.” Dr Sowatey also urged young people to take a greater interest in issues affecting the country’s security sector. He observed that although Africa has one of the world’s youngest populations and young people are increasingly active on social media and in the use of artificial intelligence, they are often left out of major national decision-making processes. He suggested that Ghana’s history of military coups may have contributed to a culture of self-censorship, discouraging citizens from questioning decisions related to national security. “The youth, when you combine the youth, social media, AI… generally, the youth are usually outside the boardroom of such major decision-making process. Maybe because of our history of coup d’etat, this feature of self-censorship and fear to ask questions.” He therefore encouraged young people to become more interested in how the security sector is managed. “I think that the other thing I’ll advise is the youth to be more interested in security sector literacy, and ask questions about military budgeting processes and expenditures.” Dr Sowatey’s final recommendation was directed at Parliament, particularly the relevant parliamentary committee responsible for defence matters. He said that legislators must consistently scrutinise military expenditure regardless of whether they are in government or in opposition. “Parliament, particularly the relevant select committee, should be more interested in asking very difficult questions. Unfortunately, it is when they are in opposition that they ask those questions.” Dr Sowatey referred to a passage from former First Lady Nana Konadu Agyeman-Rawlings’ book, It Takes a Woman: Life Shaped by Heritage, Leadership and the Woman Who Defined It. Quoting from the book, he said, “It is refreshingly amazing to uncover when a person opens his mind to non-partisan thinking, and he focuses solely on what is best for Ghana, he cannot help but see the wisdom in places and moments where he did not see it before.” Dr Sowatey said the quotation highlights the need for political leaders to put Ghana’s national interest above party considerations. He urged Parliament to adopt that mindset when exercising oversight over the defence sector. “We should get to a place where what Nana Konadu is saying comes so strongly to us, that we don’t only have those robust and genuine questions when you’re in opposition, but you also ask them when you’re in government. When we put Ghana’s security ahead of partisan interest, that is another way for Parliament to be moving ahead in the right direction.”

items.title: L’AES lance ses médias confédéraux pour renforcer sa souveraineté informationnelle
items.description: Le Burkina Faso a franchi une nouvelle etape dans la mise en place des medias communs de l'Alliance des etats du Sahel (AES). Le Parlement burkinabe a autorise, le 21 juillet, a l'unanimite des 59 deputes presents, la ratification des accords conclus avec le Mali et le Niger pour creer la radio confederale Daande Liptako et la chaîne de television Tafouk TV. Ces deux plateformes audiovisuelles ont pour objectif de renforcer la souverainete informationnelle des trois pays membres de l'AES, selon un communique officiel. Elles doivent accompagner les politiques communes dans les domaines de la securite, de l'economie et de l'integration regionale, tout en valorisant les langues, les cultures, les patrimoines et les valeurs des societes burkinabe, malienne et nigerienne. Les autorites considerent ces nouveaux medias comme des outils destines a proposer une information « credible et responsable » face aux campagnes de desinformation qu'elles denoncent regulierement. La radio Daande Liptako et Tafouk TV auront ainsi pour mission de porter la vision commune de l'AES et de renforcer la communication autour de ses projets. RT, premier partenaire international Lors des debats parlementaires, plusieurs elus ont toutefois demande des precisions sur le fonctionnement de ces structures, notamment leur financement, leur gouvernance, le recrutement des personnels, leur statut juridique ainsi que les langues qui seront utilisees a l'antenne. Le ministre burkinabe des Affaires etrangeres, Karamoko Jean Marie Traore, et le ministre de la Communication, Pingdwende Gilbert Ouedraogo, ont indique que ces aspects seraient definis progressivement lors de la mise en œuvre des projets. La creation de ces medias s'inscrit dans la volonte affichee par le Burkina Faso, le Mali et le Niger de developper des instruments communs depuis la creation de l'AES, organisation nee de leur rapprochement politique et securitaire. Les trois pays cherchent notamment a disposer de leurs propres canaux de communication afin de mieux diffuser leurs positions sur les enjeux regionaux et internationaux. Dans cette perspective, Tafouk TV a deja noue un partenariat avec la chaîne RT . a Niamey, les deux medias ont signe un protocole d'accord portant sur l'echange de contenus, le partage d'expertise et la couverture conjointe d'evenements majeurs en Russie et dans les pays du Sahel. Cet accord fait de RT le premier partenaire international de la future chaîne commune de l'AES et renforce ainsi la cooperation mediatique entre la Russie et la Confederation sahelienne.
