items.title: John Boadu backs Wontumi, urges party supporters to remain calm after conviction
items.description: Former General Secretary of the New Patriotic Party (NPP), John Boadu, has thrown his weight behind the party’s Ashanti Regional Chairman, Bernard Antwi-Boasiako, popularly known as Chairman Wontumi, describing him as a “Political Prisoner” following his conviction and imprisonment over illegal mining activities. In a press statement issued on July 22, Mr Boadu expressed sympathy for Chairman Wontumi and urged members of the NPP to remain united and law-abiding as the party pursues legal avenues to challenge the High Court’s decision. The statement follows the sentencing of Chairman Wontumi by the Accra High Court, where he was handed a 20-year prison term after being found guilty of offences connected to illegal mining on the Samreboi concession in the Western Region. Justice Audrey Kocuvie-Tay, who presided over the Criminal Division of the High Court, delivered the sentence shortly after 11:40 a.m. on Monday, July 20, bringing to a close one of Ghana’s most high-profile prosecutions relating to illegal mining, popularly known as galamsey. In addition to the custodial sentence, the court imposed a fine of 10,000 penalty units, equivalent to GH¢120,000, with a default sentence of three years’ imprisonment should the fine remain unpaid. Reacting to the court’s decision, Mr Boadu said he had followed recent developments involving Chairman Wontumi “with deep concern and sympathy”, describing the Ashanti Regional Chairman as a brother and reaffirming his support. READ ALSO: Chairman Wontumi sentenced to 20 years in prison “I have followed the recent developments involving our brother, the Ashanti Regional Chairman, Bernard Antwi Boasiako (Chairman Wontumi), with deep concern and sympathy,” the statement said. The former NPP General Secretary further endorsed the party’s position that Chairman Wontumi’s incarceration is politically motivated. “The New Patriotic Party is a family, and in moments of difficulty, we stand together. I join the Party in expressing our firm belief that Chairman Wontumi is a Political Prisoner, and I stand in full solidarity with him, his family, and his supporters.” The description of Chairman Wontumi as a “Political Prisoner” reflects the position expressed by Mr Boadu and sections of the NPP. The High Court convicted and sentenced him following criminal proceedings over illegal mining offences. While criticising the circumstances surrounding Chairman Wontumi’s imprisonment, Mr Boadu appealed to party supporters to remain peaceful and disciplined. He urged members of the NPP not to allow emotions to lead to actions that could undermine public order, stressing that the party leadership would continue to pursue all lawful means available to seek relief through the courts. “I respectfully urge all NPP members and supporters to remain calm, united, disciplined, and law-abiding while the Party leadership pursues every lawful avenue to secure his earliest release. Let us rise above provocation and continue to uphold the democratic values and traditions of our party.” Mr Boadu urged members of the NPP to remain united during what he described as a difficult period for the party. “Our strength lies in our unity, resilience, and faith in justice. May God grant Chairman Wontumi strength and protection, and may truth and justice prevail,” he wrote.

items.title: Russia, IAEA hold fresh talks on safety of Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant amid renewed attacks
items.description: By Udeme Akpan, Energy Editor Russia and the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) have held another round of high-level consultations on the safety and security of the Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant (ZNPP), with Moscow alleging an escalation of attacks around the facility and urging the UN nuclear watchdog to publicly condemn the incidents. The 13th round of interagency consultations took place in Kaliningrad on July 10, 2026, and was led by ROSATOM Director General Alexey Likhachev and IAEA Director General Rafael Grossi. The Russian delegation included representatives from the country’s nuclear regulator, Rostechnadzor, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, the Russian Armed Forces’ Radiation, Chemical and Biological Protection Troops, the National Guard (Rosgvardia), and Russia’s permanent mission to international organisations in Vienna. According to ROSATOM, discussions centred on nuclear safety and security at the Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant, Europe’s largest nuclear facility, which has remained at the centre of the Russia-Ukraine conflict since Russian forces took control of the site in 2022. Likhachev claimed that attacks around the plant, the nearby city of Energodar and surrounding areas had intensified since the end of April, alleging that Ukrainian forces were increasingly targeting the area and intimidating plant workers. He said the reported attacks had resulted in seven deaths, including one employee of the nuclear plant, while 40 others had been injured. The ROSATOM chief called on the IAEA to publicly assess what he described as repeated attacks on the facility, arguing that the absence of international condemnation could encourage further actions that threaten nuclear safety. The consultations also reviewed the status of electricity supply to the plant following repairs to the high-voltage Dneprovskaya transmission line. According to the Russian side, significant repair work was carried out by the Ministry of Defence, Rosgvardia, Rosseti specialists and plant personnel to restore the line, although Moscow said it had yet to be re-energised from the Ukrainian-controlled side. Another key issue discussed was the continued deployment of IAEA experts at the facility and arrangements for their regular rotation, as well as measures to improve reporting on conditions at the plant and in Energodar. Both sides agreed on the importance of maintaining regular dialogue on issues relating to the safety and security of the nuclear power station. The Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant has remained one of the most sensitive facilities in the conflict, with both Russia and Ukraine repeatedly accusing each other of attacks that could endanger nuclear safety. The IAEA has consistently called for restraint by all parties and has maintained a permanent monitoring mission at the site. The post Russia, IAEA hold fresh talks on safety of Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant amid renewed attacks appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Foreign Affairs Minister welcomes Burundi President, AU officials for 2026 Accra health summit
items.description: The Minister for Foreign Affairs, Hon. Samuel Okudzeto Ablakwa, has received the President of the Republic of Burundi and Chairperson of the African Union, H.E. Évariste Ndayishimiye; the Deputy Prime Minister of the Federal Democratic Republic of Ethiopia, H.E. Temesgen Tiruneh; the Chairperson of the African Union Commission, H.E. Mahmoud Ali Youssouf. Also, other ministers and senior officials from across the continent, who have arrived in Ghana to participate in the Extraordinary Session of the African Union Assembly on Ending AIDS by 2030 and Addressing Preventable Maternal Deaths, Communicable and Non-Communicable Diseases. The two-day summit will also focus on tackling preventable maternal deaths, communicable and non-communicable diseases prevalent across the continent, while advancing strategies to strengthen Africa’s health systems by the end of the decade. The Extraordinary Summit, taking place in Accra from July 21 and 22, 2026, brings together African leaders and key stakeholders to reinforce the continent’s commitment to ending AIDS by 2030, reducing preventable maternal deaths, and accelerating coordinated action against communicable and non-communicable diseases.

items.title: Fernández Díaz alega que se busca condenarlo en Kitchen “por razón” de su cargo de ministro
items.description: Jorge Fernández Díaz, ministro del Interior de 2011 a 2016 y hombre de confianza del expresidente Mariano Rajoy, ya ha jugado sus cartas en el juicio del caso Kitchen , que se ha reanudado este miércoles en la Audiencia Nacional. Tres meses después del arranque de la vista , su abogado ha expuesto al tribunal su alegato final y ha aprovechado para desmarcarlo de la puesta en marcha de la operación de espionaje al extesorero popular Luis Bárcenas, que tenía como supuesto objetivo robarle documentos comprometedores que pudiera guardar de cargos del partido para que no llegaran así a los investigadores del caso Gürtel . La defensa ha reprochado que se trata de endosar al exdirigente del PP una “responsabilidad penal” por el mero hecho de que ejercía el cargo de ministro; y ha cargado contra Francisco Martínez, ex secretario de Estado de Seguridad y su antigua mano derecha, que implicó a su exjefe en la trama durante la fase de instrucción. Seguir leyendo

items.title: World’s biggest YouTuber MrBeast weds partner on private island
items.description: American YouTube sensation Jimmy Donaldson, popularly known as MrBeast, has married content creator and author Thea Booysen in an intimate ceremony held in the Caribbean. The 28-year-old, who is the world’s most-subscribed YouTuber with over 500 million subscribers, announced the wedding on Instagram, where he shared a collection of photographs capturing different moments from the celebration. The images showed the newlyweds in several outfits throughout the event, including their traditional wedding attire. Donaldson wore a tailored suit, while Booysen appeared in a long lace wedding dress before later changing into a shorter gown. The couple also posed in coordinated white casual outfits believed to have been worn during the reception. Sharing his joy with fans, Donaldson described the occasion as the happiest day of his life. “I found Mrs Beast and it was the best day of my life,” he wrote. According to reports, the wedding was held on British billionaire Richard Branson’s private Necker Island in the Caribbean. Around 70 family members and close friends attended the week-long celebration, which reportedly began on July 14. Before the couple exchanged vows, guests enjoyed activities including snorkelling and kitesurfing. The private ceremony was officiated by a family friend who is an ordained minister. Donaldson and Booysen’s relationship began after they met during a trip to South Africa in 2022. Booysen had been invited by a mutual friend to join the YouTube star for dinner, and the pair later started dating. The couple got engaged on Christmas Day in 2024. The post World’s biggest YouTuber MrBeast weds partner on private island appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Why I appointed Omenugha as COOU Vice-Chancellor — Soludo
items.description: By Vincent Ujumadu Anambra State Governor, Prof. Chukwuma Soludo, has explained that merit, experience and proven leadership informed the appointment of Prof. Kate Azuka Omenugha as the fifth substantive Vice-Chancellor of Chukwuemeka Odumegwu Ojukwu University (COOU), Igbariam. Speaking at a thanksgiving Mass held at St. Paul Catholic Church, Nnobi, to mark Omenugha’s first anniversary in office as Vice-Chancellor and the 70th birthday of her husband, Dr. Michael Omenugha, the governor said she emerged as the most qualified candidate for the position. Soludo said Omenugha’s outstanding record in the education sector, particularly her firm stance against examination malpractice during her tenure as Anambra State Commissioner for Education, distinguished her from other contenders. He recalled that under her leadership as commissioner, the state government shut down more than 486 so-called “miracle centres” involved in examination malpractice, describing the move as a demonstration of courage, integrity and commitment to educational excellence. According to the governor, Omenugha embodies the quality of leadership required to advance his administration’s vision of transforming Anambra into a smart, digital, livable and prosperous state by 2070. Soludo also disclosed that he came under pressure from influential individuals to appoint another candidate from the university’s professorial community but resisted the lobbying in favour of what he described as the best-qualified person for the job. “I refused to succumb to pressure because the interest of the university and the state was paramount,” the governor said, stressing that merit remains the guiding principle for appointments under his administration. The thanksgiving service and birthday celebration were attended by government officials, political associates, academics, members of the clergy, family members and other well-wishers. The post Why I appointed Omenugha as COOU Vice-Chancellor — Soludo appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: ‎Banfora : La Brigade nationale de sapeurs-pompiers repêche trois corps sans vie ‎
items.description: ‎La Cinquième Compagnie d'Incendie et de Secours de Banfora est intervenue à deux reprises, le mardi 21 juillet 2026, pour des opérations de repêchage de corps sans vie dans la région des Tannouyan, informe la Brigade sur sa page Facebook. La première intervention a eu lieu dans le village de Sipigui, situé dans la commune de Orodara à environ 45 kilomètres de Banfora. Les sapeurs-pompiers ont procédé au repêchage du corps sans vie d'une femme enceinte tombée dans un puits. ‎Quelques heures plus tard, une seconde opération a été menée dans le village de Sarékadougou, localité située à une vingtaine de kilomètres de Banfora. Cette intervention a permis de repêcher les corps sans vie de deux enfants retrouvés dans une étendue d'eau. À la suite de ces drames, la Brigade nationale de sapeurs-pompiers (BNSP) appelle les populations à une vigilance renforcée aux abords des puits, des plans d'eau et des zones humides. Elle recommande notamment la sécurisation des puits à l'aide de dispositifs de protection adaptés afin de prévenir les chutes accidentelles et les noyades. ‎La BNSP rappelle que l'adoption de mesures de prévention simples peut contribuer à sauver des vies, particulièrement en cette période où les points d'eau sont davantage fréquentés et où les risques liés aux noyades demeurent élevés. ‎ ‎ ‎Lefaso.net ‎Source : Page Facebook de la Brigade nationale des sapeurs-pompiers

items.title: Rubio says he discussed China President's US visit in meeting with Wang Yi
items.description: By Michael Martina MANILA, July 22 (Reuters) - U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said on Wednesday he talked a lot during a meeting with Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi about a planned trip to the United States by President Xi Jinping. Meeting with Wang in Manila on the sidelines of a regional gathering led by the Southeast Asian bloc ASEAN, Rubio told reporters that both sides had differences, but their job was to manage them, adding there were areas of potential cooperation. President Donald Trump has said a summit with Xi could take place at the end of September.

items.title: FG, Japan, UNESCO launch digital teacher training for 2,000 educators
items.description: …Boost Female School Leadership The Federal Government has launched a three-day Master Training Workshop aimed at enhancing teachers’ professional development through mobile-based learning, with a target of training at least 2,000 teachers and school leaders across Nigeria. The initiative, which places special emphasis on empowering female school leaders, is being implemented by the Federal Ministry of Education in collaboration with the Government of Japan, UNESCO-International Institute for Capacity Building in Africa (UNESCO-IICBA), the Pan-African University (PAU) and Castalia Co. Ltd. under the Pan-African Virtual and Electronic University (PAVEU) Initiative. Speaking at the opening of the workshop, the Minister of State for Education, Professor Suwaiba Sa’id Ahmad, represented by the Director of Colleges of Education, Dr. Uchenna Uba, described the programme as a significant step towards improving teacher quality and advancing digital innovation in Nigeria’s education sector. She said the initiative aligns with the Nigeria Education Sector Renewal Initiative (NESRI) and is designed to strengthen teaching capacity, improve learning outcomes and expand access to continuous professional development through technology. The workshop is equipping selected master trainers with digital competencies that will enable them to train teachers and school administrators nationwide using mobile-based learning platforms. Participants are receiving instruction in digital literacy, the application of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in education, gender-responsive pedagogy, mental health, learner-centred teaching methodologies and the use of the GOKU Mobile Learning Platform. As part of its support for the programme, the Government of Japan is investing approximately US$1 million to drive implementation. Development partners also donated 40 tablets and two edge computing devices to facilitate the nationwide rollout of the initiative. The Federal Ministry of Education reaffirmed its commitment to leveraging digital technology and strategic partnerships to provide inclusive, equitable and quality education, noting that strengthening teachers’ capacity remains critical to improving learning outcomes for Nigerian children. The post FG, Japan, UNESCO launch digital teacher training for 2,000 educators appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: La tercera ola de calor del verano alcanza su cénit
items.description: ¿Es este miércoles el peor día de la nueva ola de calor ? Pues, como todo en la vida, depende desde dónde se mire. En el conjunto de España, “será el día más cálido en general, aunque el jueves será especialmente caluroso en las regiones mediterráneas, donde repuntarán las temperaturas”, explica Rubén del Campo, portavoz de la Agencia Estatal de Meteorología (Aemet). Tanto, que la Aemet ha activado en las últimas horas para el jueves el aviso rojo, el máximo, en Murcia y en la Comunidad Valenciana . Para este miércoles son 12 las comunidades bajo aviso, aunque ninguno rojo, por temperaturas que van de 39° a 42°. Seguir leyendo

La première intervention a concerné le repêchage du corps sans vie d'une femme enceinte dans un puits au village de Sipigui, dans la commune d'Orodara, localité située à environ 45 km de Banfora. La Brigade Nationale de Sapeurs-Pompiers invite les populations à faire preuve d'une extrême prudence à proximité des puits, des étendues d'eau et des zones humides à haut risques et sécuriser les puits par des dispositifs de protection adaptés afin de prévenir les risques de noyade. items.title: Atiku demands joint summon of OAGF, CBN Gov over conflicting PFIPC accounts
items.description: By Omeiza Ajayi ABUJA:Former Vice President Atiku Abubakar has called on the National Assembly to immediately summon the Accountant-General of the Federation and the Governor of the Central Bank of Nigeria CBN to appear together before its investigative committee under oath, so that Nigerians can finally hear one truthful account of the controversial Presidential Foreign Investment Promotion Council PFIPC accounts scandal. In a statement issued Wednesday by his Senior Special Assistant on Public Communication, Phrank Shaibu, the Presidential Candidate of the African Democratic Congress ADC said the demand became necessary following conflicting testimonies by the Office of the Accountant-General of the Federation OAGF and the CBN before the House of Representatives, which he said had exposed a government trapped by its own contradictions. Atiku said the contradictory accounts pointed to the beginning of a cover-up, noting that both institutions could not simultaneously be telling the truth. “This is where the cover-up appears to begin. One institution denied authorising the opening of the PFIPC accounts and alleged forgery. Another, before the representatives of the Nigerian people, stated that the accounts were opened on the directive of the Accountant-General after due process was followed. Both accounts cannot be true,” he said. He said the conflicting testimonies had plunged the Tinubu administration into one of the gravest credibility crises in Nigeria’s democratic history, adding that he could only sympathise with President Bola Ahmed Tinubu over the situation. “When two of your most sensitive financial and monetary authorities publicly contradict each other on the same transaction, no serious leader needs a clairvoyant to know that his administration is trapped in a web of deception and a profound crisis of credibility. The President cannot continue to watch from the sidelines while his government tears itself apart with conflicting narratives,” Atiku said. He insisted that Tinubu must clarify which of the two accounts he believed, warning that continued inaction deepened public suspicion that image management had taken precedence over the truth. “President Tinubu must answer a simple question: Which version does he believe—the Accountant-General’s or the Central Bank’s? If he believes neither, why has he not acted? Every day he delays ordering a truly independent investigation, he deepens public suspicion that preserving his administration’s image has taken precedence over establishing the truth,” he said. Atiku also faulted the continued silence of the Attorney-General of the Federation, describing it as a factor compounding the crisis. “The continued silence of the Attorney-General of the Federation, the nation’s chief law officer, only compounds this credibility crisis and reinforces the perception that this administration is more interested in containing the political fallout than uncovering the truth,” he said. He maintained that the scandal had gone beyond a dispute over disputed accounts to become a referendum on the President’s integrity and competence, querying how many other abuses might have gone undetected because of weak institutional oversight. “The PFIPC scandal has ceased to be merely about disputed accounts. It has become a referendum on President Tinubu’s integrity and competence. If a single individual could allegedly orchestrate a scheme that has thrown two of Nigeria’s most critical financial and monetary authorities into public contradiction, Nigerians are entitled to ask how many other abuses may have escaped detection because of weak oversight and institutional failure,” he said. Atiku insisted that the president owed Nigerians an apology and had forfeited the moral authority to remain in office if he failed to act. “President Tinubu owes Nigerians an unreserved apology for presiding over this unprecedented collapse of public accountability. If he cannot immediately restore public confidence through a genuinely independent investigation and ensure that everyone responsible is held to account, then he has forfeited the moral authority to continue in office. The honourable course is simple: apologise to Nigerians and resign.” The post Atiku demands joint summon of OAGF, CBN Gov over conflicting PFIPC accounts appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: «Vos trajets quotidiens sont plus dangereux que mon ancien métier»
items.description: Jean Todt a passé des décennies au cœur du sport automobile le plus dangereux au monde. Copilote de rallye, directeur de l'écurie Ferrari en Formule 1, puis président de l'instance dirigeante mondiale du sport automobile, il a connu une époque où la mort d'un pilote sur un circuit faisait à peine les gros titres. «Quand j'ai commencé, très jeune, c'était extrêmement dangereux», raconte-t-il. «Les gens n'étaient pas surpris si quelqu'un se faisait tuer dans une voiture de course.» Ce monde n'existe plus. Des décennies de progrès en matière d'ingénierie, de réglementation et de sécurité ont rendu survivables des collisions à plus de 320 km/h. «Aujourd'hui, tout le monde serait surpris si quelqu'un était blessé dans une voiture de course», souligne M. Todt. Il souhaite désormais voir cette même transformation s'appliquer aux trajets du quotidien — qu'il s'agisse d'aller à l'école, de se rendre au travail à moto ou de livreurs qui se faufilent dans la circulation — car les chiffres enregistrés sur les routes ordinaires sont encore ceux que connaissait autrefois la course automobile. ONU Jean Todt lors d'une visite au Kirghizistan. Une pandémie silencieuse sur les routes Chaque année, 1,2 million de personnes sont tuées sur les routes dans le monde. Cinquante millions d'autres sont blessées. Les accidents de la circulation constituent la première cause de décès chez les enfants et les jeunes âgés de 5 à 29 ans. Un bilan si constant que Jean Todt, aujourd'hui Envoyé spécial du Secrétaire général de l'ONU pour la sécurité routière, parle d'«une pandémie silencieuse sur les routes». «Malheureusement, on finit par s'habituer à cette situation», déplore-t-il. Les dirigeants mondiaux et des ministres se réunissent lundi et mardi au siège des Nations Unies, à New York, pour une réunion de deux jours visant à réduire de moitié le nombre de décès sur les routes d'ici à 2030, un objectif que le monde est aujourd'hui loin d'atteindre. Selon M. Todt, ce qui rend cette crise particulière, c'est que personne ne manque de solutions. «Contrairement à d'autres pandémies, nous avons les remèdes : l'éducation, l'application des lois, la qualité des véhicules, la qualité des routes et la qualité de la prise en charge après un accident», explique-t-il. Selon lui, si les règles concernant le port de la ceinture de sécurité, du casque, les limitations de vitesse et la conduite en état d'ivresse étaient correctement appliquées, « nous diviserions immédiatement par deux le nombre de victimes sur les routes ». Près de 90 % des décès sur les routes surviennent dans les pays à revenu faible ou intermédiaire, où des routes plus sûres, des lois plus strictes et une véritable application de celles-ci restent encore hors de portée. Commencer dès le plus jeune âge Pour l'Envoyé spécial, tout commence par l'éducation. « Si l'on éduque un jeune garçon ou une jeune fille de six, sept ou huit ans, il saura immédiatement comment se comporter », explique-t-il. Et surtout, ces enfants transmettent ensuite ces bonnes pratiques aux adultes qui les conduisent. Mais l'éducation, sans conséquences, ne mène nulle part, avertit-il. «Si les gens savent qu'ils peuvent mal se comporter sans conséquence, ils n'ont aucune raison de changer.» Quant à la corruption, ajoute-t-il sans détour, elle est «incompatible» avec une application efficace de la loi. De nouveaux risques continuent d'apparaître. Les vélos électriques, les trottinettes électriques et les livreurs travaillant via des plateformes numériques, souvent rémunérés à la course et courant contre la montre, parfois sans casque, ont ajouté de nouveaux dangers dans les rues des villes. Les voitures autonomes pourraient, à terme, contribuer à améliorer la situation, estime M. Todt, mais pas dans un avenir proche, et certainement pas là où la crise est la plus grave : dans les pays où circulent encore des parcs automobiles vieillissants et où les transports publics demeurent insuffisants. © JCDecaux Circuit de sécurité routière « Golden Road » en Colombie Faire entendre la sécurité routière Même au sein de sa propre institution, Jean Todt estime que la sécurité routière peine à se faire entendre. «Très souvent, je voyage, je rencontre l'équipe des Nations Unies et je dis: « Je vais être le premier à parler de sécurité routière. » Très souvent, j'ai raison.» Son message, formulé sans jargon, est celui qui a transformé son ancien sport : les décès n'ont jamais été une fatalité. Ceux de la route ne le sont pas davantage. «La prévention est essentielle», insiste-t-il. Il a fallu des décennies à la course automobile pour intégrer cette réalité. Jean Todt espère que les routes n'auront pas besoin d'attendre aussi longtemps. Source: ONU Info L’article «Vos trajets quotidiens sont plus dangereux que mon ancien métier» est apparu en premier sur Wakat Séra .

items.title: Italian FA chief says in touch with Guardiola over coaching vacancy
items.description: The head of Italy’s football federation has confirmed he held discussions with legendary coach Pep Guardiola over the vacancy at the Italian national side but added he was “not sure that this will come about”. Giovanni Malago revealed the talks had taken place in an interview with the website Cronache di Spogliatoio (Dressing Room Chronicles) published on Wednesday. Asked whether the budget for the coach’s salary could be increased in the case of Guardiola, he said: “Yes, and that is the exception for obvious reasons”. “I won’t go into detail here but it’s not sure that this will come about. “However, I still believe it was the right thing to do. It was important to open a dialogue and keep it alive,” he added. Guardiola has first hand knowledge of Italian football having played for Brescia and AS Roma (2001-03). Already at a low ebb, Italian football was sent into a tailspin in March after the men’s national team failed for a third consecutive time to qualify for the World Cup. Italy has won four World Cups. Guardiola, 55, stepped down at Manchester City at the end of last season after winning six Premier League titles and the Champions League in his glittering decade at the Etihad Stadium. According to Italian media reports, former Italy internationals Andrea Pirlo and Roberto Mancini — who guided the Azzurri to the Euro 2020 title — are also being considered as candidates to coach the national team, which faces Belgium on September 25 in the Nations League. The post Italian FA chief says in touch with Guardiola over coaching vacancy appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: EduSpots selected among 31 organisations worldwide for FIFA Global Citizen Education Fund
items.description: EduSpots has been selected as one of 31 organisations worldwide to join the second cohort of the FIFA Global Citizen Education Fund. The Fund supports community-based organisations that are expanding access to quality education and sport for children and young people in underserved communities. EduSpots enables communities to create the future they want to see through education, active citizenship and play. The organisation is excited to partner with Global Citizen and FIFA over the coming year to strengthen community-led, inclusive and joyful learning across its nationwide network. Thanks to this support, EduSpots will train 100 community educators (“Catalysts”) through high-quality residential leadership academies, with a focus on inclusive teaching methodologies. The organisation will also engage 10,000 learners (“Sparks”) in community-led clubs spanning early years education, digital literacy, STEM, environmental action and gender equity, supported by feedback and recognition systems through the EduSpots App. In addition, EduSpots will support 52 community-owned Spots to establish sports clubs, choosing from football, basketball, volleyball and table tennis, backed by local grant funding and ongoing mentoring. All 52 Spots will receive additional books and learning resources to strengthen educational opportunities. The organisation will also strengthen its education innovations by launching a new Edu-Animation channel, drawing on ideas generated by local Spots. EduSpots’ Catalyst Committee Chair, Nimatu Abdul-Rahman, said the organisation was honoured to be selected for the global initiative. “The whole EduSpots network is incredibly excited to have been selected for this remarkable opportunity to work with FIFA and Global Citizen. This partnership will help us deepen and expand our community-led education model, enabling even more young people to develop the skills, knowledge and mindset to become active citizens in their own communities.” EduSpots’ Patron, Professor Elsie Effah Kaufmann, described the selection as recognition of the collective efforts of everyone involved with the organisation. “This recognition is a testament to the power of synergy, how small but meaningful contributions from like-minded individuals can lead to massive impact. Ayekoo to all the EduSpots Sparks, Catalysts and leadership. I can’t wait to see the great things you will do with this opportunity.” EduSpots expressed appreciation to Global Citizen and FIFA for the opportunity and also thanked its network of Catalysts, whose dedication and leadership continue to drive the organisation’s work across Ghana. More information about the Fund and the other selected organisations is available at http://globalcitizen.org/education-fund-grantees . About the FIFA Global Citizen Education Fund The FIFA Global Citizen Education Fund is a global initiative aimed at expanding access to quality education and football for children in more than 200 communities worldwide. FIFA and Global Citizen launched the Fund to mobilise USD $100 million by the 2026 FIFA World Cup Final. EduSpots is among the beneficiaries receiving grants ranging from USD $50,000 to USD $250,000 to expand access to and improve children’s education. The organisation is proud to receive the grant alongside organisations from nine countries. To date, the Fund has raised more than USD $30 million through contributions from philanthropies, major brands and corporations, individual donors, and proceeds from ticket sales to major music and sporting events. About Global Citizen Global Citizen is the world’s largest movement dedicated to ending extreme poverty. Its impact is driven by an international community of everyday advocates who take action and amplified by campaigns that bring together leaders in music, entertainment, public policy, media, philanthropy and the private sector. To date, more than USD $50 billion in commitments announced through Global Citizen platforms has been deployed, positively impacting 1.3 billion lives. About EduSpots EduSpots is a Ghanaian and UK-registered NGO advancing digitalised, community-led education through a growing network of community-owned learning hubs known as “Spots”. Its work is led by more than 400 local changemakers, known as Catalysts, who establish and manage Spots in their own communities while facilitating inclusive education clubs and developing as educational leaders. In 2026, more than 10,000 learners of all ages will access Spots, benefiting from books, digital technology, locally relevant resources and future-ready learning opportunities. EduSpots combines community leadership with digital innovation through the EduSpots App and its unique WhatsApp for Learning model, enabling daily peer learning, collaboration and professional development across the network.

items.title: Kaolack : Guedji Diouf officiellement installé gouverneur
items.description: Guedji Diouf est officiellement le nouveau gouverneur de la région de Kaolack. Il a été installé hier dans ses fonctions au cours d'une cérémonie présidée par le ministre de l'Intérieur, Mouhamadou Makhtar Cissé. Il succède à Mouhamadou Moctar Watt, nommé directeur de cabinet du ministre de l'Intérieur. Devant les autorités administratives, les élus territoriaux, les chefs religieux et coutumiers ainsi que de nombreuses personnalités, le nouveau gouverneur a exprimé sa gratitude au chef de l'État pour la confiance placée en lui. Conscient des enjeux liés à une région au fort potentiel économique, il a promis d'exercer sa mission dans un esprit de dialogue, de concertation et de proximité. Guedji Diouf a indiqué que son action portera notamment sur l'accompagnement du développement local ainsi que sur la promotion de l'entrepreneuriat des jeunes et des femmes. Sollicitant le concours des services de l'État et les prières des guides religieux, il a assuré vouloir répondre aux attentes des populations. Passant le témoin, Mouhamadou Moctar Watt a invité son successeur à poursuivre la dynamique engagée, soulignant le caractère stratégique de Kaolack et les perspectives qu'offre notamment l'ouverture de l'autoroute Fatick-Kaolack. Il a également remercié le ministre de l'Intérieur pour la confiance qui lui a été renouvelée à travers sa nomination comme directeur de cabinet. Présidant la cérémonie, le ministre de l'Intérieur a salué le parcours de Mouhamadou Moctar Watt au sein de l'administration territoriale avant d'exhorter Guedji Diouf à renforcer la coordination de l'action publique, l'aménagement urbain et la promotion de l’emploi, avec l'appui de l'ensemble des autorités administratives. Mamadou THIAM ( Correspondant)

items.title: Ghana becomes first African country to sign accelerated patent grant deal with US
items.description: The United States Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO) has signed an Accelerated Patent Grant (APG) arrangement with Ghana’s intellectual property office (GHIPO), the Registrar General’s Department, and the Ministry of Justice. The agreement is the first of its kind between the USPTO and an African country. Under Secretary of Commerce and USPTO Director John A. Squires and the Registrar-General of Ghana, Grace Issahaque, signed the statement of intent on July 9 in Geneva, Switzerland, on the margins of the annual meeting of the World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO). The agreement with Ghana is the tenth such arrangement signed by the USPTO and a partner intellectual property office. “The USPTO is committed to building its relationships with IP offices in Africa,” said Under Secretary Squires in a statement on the USPTO website. “With the signing of this agreement with Ghana, we are building on that commitment, which took a big step forward last year with the installation of the USPTO’s first-ever intellectual property attaché for the region, Katherine Hiner, in Johannesburg, South Africa.” Under the APG process, an eligible patent applicant who has been granted a U.S. patent by the USPTO may request that Ghana’s IP office grant a patent on a corresponding Ghanaian patent application pending before GHIPO. This worksharing arrangement will continue for five years from the date of signing. Guidelines describing GHIPO’s implementation of the programme and the process for how a U.S. patent holder can take advantage of the arrangement will soon be available on GHIPO’s website. Following the historic signing, Under Secretary Squires delivered welcome remarks at a WIPO Assemblies side event with the U.S. Mission and Universal Music Group, “IP for Growth Initiative.” Speaking at a livestreamed panel discussion and performance featuring African musicians and U.S. artist John Legend, USPTO Attaché Katherine Hiner detailed the role that the USPTO can play in empowering creators in Africa. “In today’s global marketplace, rights holders need to have good, consistent laws that protect IP across borders,” she said, adding that “this includes recruiting and training staff, procurement of the necessary equipment, ongoing review of procedures, and facilitating an open dialogue with rights holders in their markets.” With a gross domestic product of $114 billion in 2025, Ghana is one of the faster-growing economies in Africa. In 2024, according to WIPO, 33 per cent of foreign patent applications in Ghana originated with U.S.-based filers. The agreement is expected to significantly improve the efficiency of Ghana’s patent examination process by reducing processing time while maintaining the country’s intellectual property standards.

items.title: France : le Parlement interdit les réseaux sociaux aux moins de 15 ans et offre une victoire à Macron
items.description: L'interdiction des reseaux sociaux aux moins de 15 ans, adoptee par une large majorite de parlementaires, entend proteger la jeunesse des dangers des plateformes numeriques, tout en etendant l'interdiction des telephones portables au lycee. Son adoption a suscite de vives inquietudes parmi les internautes et irrite plusieurs soutiens du Rassemblement national. Apres la loi sur la fin de vie, les parlementaires offrent ainsi une nouvelle victoire a Emmanuel Macron. Le dispositif repose sur une verification de l'âge imposee aux plateformes, sans sanctions pour les mineurs ni leurs parents. « Nous allons, nous Francais, tous devoir prouver notre âge pendant quatre mois », a explique la ministre deleguee au Numerique, Anne Le Henanff. Le systeme technique n'est toutefois pas encore pleinement au point, alors que la France connaît de grandes difficultes pour proteger les donnees des internautes . ✅ Adoption de la proposition de loi visant à protéger les mineurs des risques auxquels les expose l’utilisation des réseaux sociaux, compte tenu du texte de la commission mixte paritaire 🔎En savoir plus : https://t.co/cbn9v5ckiK #DirectAN pic.twitter.com/rxifsABqjx — Assemblée nationale (@AssembleeNat) July 21, 2026 Les parlementaires ayant adopte le texte ont offert une victoire symbolique a Emmanuel Macron, qui a salue « une avancee majeure » et estime que la France « ouvre la voie » en Europe. L'Australie avait toutefois deja emprunte ce chemin, tandis que le Royaume-Uni pourrait suivre le mouvement . Le Rassemblement national s'est abstenu lors du vote definitif. Auparavant, lors des premieres lectures, le groupe RN avait plutot vote en faveur de cette mesure de restriction, s'attirant les foudres d'une partie de ses soutiens sur internet. Parmi les plus virulents figure Pierre Sautarel, fondateur du site Fdesouche, qui a fustige la position du RN et souligne la concomitance de la mesure avec le calendrier electoral, a l'approche de l'election presidentielle de 2027. La loi est prévue pour entrer en vigueur le 1er septembre 2026 pour les nouveaux comptes. Pour les comptes déjà existants, elle ne s’appliquera qu’après un délai de quatre mois, soit à partir du 1er janvier 2027. L’élection présidentielle aura lieu les 18 avril 2027 pour le… — Pierre Sautarel (@FrDesouche) July 21, 2026 Les socialistes ont exprime de fortes reserves concernant la vie privee et la liberte d'information. Le depute Arthur Delaporte a notamment juge la mesure « quasi inapplicable ». La France insoumise a, de son cote, vote contre. Le depute Louis Boyard a denonce « une immense part d'internet que vous allez soumettre a un controle d'identite » et annonce une saisine du Conseil constitutionnel. Il rejoint ainsi les preoccupations exprimees au Senat, majoritairement a droite, concernant un possible risque de censure. L’Assemblée nationale vient de voter l’obligation de faire vérifier votre pièce d’identité pour vous connecter à vos réseaux sociaux. Cela sera piloté par la Commission européenne. Le RN s’est abstenu. C’est la fin de l’anonymat sur Internet. Nous déposons un recours devant le… pic.twitter.com/CwW5jbFZSO — Louis Boyard (@LouisBoyard) July 21, 2026 Le texte prevoit des exceptions pour les encyclopedies en ligne et les projets educatifs. YouTube et WhatsApp ne seront concernes que pour certaines de leurs fonctionnalites sociales. Les debats ont egalement porte sur les risques de contournement et sur l'absence de regulation des algorithmes, cette question etant renvoyee a l'echelon europeen.

items.title: Mahama, former presidents, other dignitaries to attend Apostle Kwadwo Safo’s funeral – Family confirms
items.description: The family of the late Apostle Kwadwo Safo has announced that final preparations for his burial and funeral rites have reached their final stages, with the family, children and leadership of the Kristo Asafo Mission united in their determination to accord the revered spiritual leader, industrialist, inventor, technologist, farmer and philanthropist a befitting farewell. In a statement issued by the family, it said that following initial discussions surrounding succession matters, all differences had been put behind them and that they were now singularly focused on celebrating the extraordinary life and enduring legacy of one of Ghana’s greatest innovators, visionaries and nation-builders According to the family, the burial ceremony will take place on Thursday, July 30, 2026, at the Kantanka Automobile Manufacturing Centre at Gomoa Mpota in the Central Region—the industrial hub established by the late Apostle Kwadwo Safo, where his vision of a wholly Ghanaian automobile manufacturing industry took shape. In accordance with his last testament, Apostle Kwadwo Safo left detailed instructions on how he wished to be remembered by future generations. In fulfilment of those wishes, a memorial edifice has been constructed within the industrial complex to preserve his legacy. The memorial will house his personal effects, including his attire, footwear, tools, inventions, prototypes and selected works, creating a permanent repository of his life’s achievements. The family announced that the national funeral service will be held on Friday, July 31, 2026, at the Black Star Square (Independence Square) in Accra, where tens of thousands of mourners from across Ghana and abroad are expected to gather to pay their final respects. The funeral is expected to attract an unprecedented gathering of dignitaries from across the political, traditional, religious, diplomatic and business communities. President John Dramani Mahama is expected to lead the nation’s tribute, accompanied by Chief of Staff Julius Debrah, ministers of state and senior government officials. Former Presidents John Agyekum Kufuor and Nana Addo Dankwa Akufo-Addo have also expressed their willingness to attend the funeral in honour of the late Apostle Kwadwo Safo, the family said. Joining them will be former Vice President and Flagbearer of the New Patriotic Party, Dr Mahamudu Bawumia, together with national executives of both the NPP and NDC. The Speaker of Parliament, Rt. Hon. Alban Sumana Kingsford Bagbin, together with Members of Parliament from both the Majority and Minority Caucuses, are also expected to attend. In a rare parliamentary honour, the Speaker has directed that a statement be made on the floor of Parliament in memory of the late Apostle Kwadwo Safo, with members from both sides expected to pay tribute to his extraordinary contributions to Ghana’s industrial, scientific and technological advancement. As part of Parliament’s tribute, Parliament TV will throughout the week broadcast a special documentary chronicling the remarkable life, inventions and achievements of Apostle Kwadwo Safo. The final funeral observances will conclude with a Thanksgiving Service on Saturday, August 8, 2026, at the Prempeh Assembly Hall in Kumasi, the family added. The family said Apostle Kwadwo Safo leaves behind an unparalleled legacy of innovation, self-reliance, industrial excellence and service to humanity. Through his pioneering work in automobile manufacturing, engineering, agriculture, education, science and technology, he demonstrated that Africans possess the ingenuity and capacity to solve their own developmental challenges. His life’s work remains an enduring testament to Ghanaian creativity and resilience and will continue to inspire generations to come.

Statut particulier, hausse salariale : la CDT accuse El Midaoui de bloquer le dialogue social
items.description: Le climat social se tend dans le secteur de l'enseignement supérieur. Le Syndicat national des fonctionnaires de l'enseignement supérieur et des cités universitaires, affilié à la Confédération démocratique du travail (CDT), accuse le ministre de l'Enseignement supérieur, de la Recherche scientifique et de l'Innovation, Azzedine El Midaoui, de maintenir le dialogue social dans l'impasse. En cause, selon l'organisation syndicale, le retard pris dans l'adoption du statut particulier des fonctionnaires du secteur, le non-versement de l'augmentation salariale de 1.000 dirhams annoncée par le ministre et l'absence de réponses aux autres revendications professionnelles. Cette montée des tensions s'est traduite, le 14 juillet, par une grève nationale organisée par le syndicat, qui réclame l'accélération de la publication du statut particulier des fonctionnaires de l'enseignement supérieur, le paiement de la hausse salariale promise ainsi qu'un renforcement des effectifs afin d'alléger la charge de travail pesant sur les personnels administratifs des établissements et des services relevant du ministère. Dans un communiqué, le syndicat affirme que les fonctionnaires « restent attachés à leurs revendications légitimes », citant en priorité « l'adoption rapide d'un statut particulier juste et équitable, le respect de l'ensemble des engagements antérieurs et la satisfaction des revendications professionnelles et sociales urgentes ». Pour Abdelhak Hissan, dirigeant du Syndicat national des fonctionnaires de l'enseignement supérieur et des cités universitaires, l'origine du conflit réside avant tout dans le refus du ministère d'engager un dialogue avec son organisation. « Notre syndicat a arrêté un programme de mobilisation après le refus du ministère d'ouvrir le dialogue. Nous avions déposé une demande de rencontre dès la mi-mai », explique-t-il. Selon lui, le ministère a reçu plusieurs organisations syndicales, « qu'elles soient représentatives ou non », tout en écartant la CDT, pourtant « première organisation du secteur » et « syndicat le plus représentatif ». « Au lieu de recevoir notre syndicat, qui avait officiellement sollicité une rencontre, le ministre a préféré inviter toutes les autres organisations. Il a évité de rencontrer la CDT », a-t-il confié à la presse. Le responsable syndical estime que le principal dossier en souffrance demeure celui du statut particulier des fonctionnaires de l'enseignement supérieur, élaboré, selon lui, en concertation avec les partenaires sociaux. « Ce texte a été finalisé depuis janvier 2025 avec une importante contribution de notre syndicat. Pourtant, il n'a toujours pas été publié, alors que d'autres secteurs disposent déjà de leurs nouveaux statuts », affirme-t-il. Face à ce retard, poursuit-il, le ministre avait proposé une augmentation de 1.000 dirhams en attendant la publication du statut. Une promesse qui, selon la CDT, n'a jamais été concrétisée. « Ni l'augmentation n'a été versée, ni le statut particulier n'a vu le jour, et aucun des autres problèmes n'a été réglé », regrette Hissan. Le syndicat critique également la manière dont le ministère met en œuvre la loi 54.23, estimant avoir été tenu à l'écart de son processus d'application. Il s'inquiète notamment d'un projet de décret relatif aux conditions d'accès au poste de directeur administratif et financier, une fonction qu'il considère comme exclusivement administrative. « Le projet de décret prévoit pourtant d'ouvrir ce poste aux enseignants-chercheurs », déplore le responsable syndical, estimant que cette orientation ne correspond pas aux missions de ces derniers. Selon lui, les enseignants-chercheurs devraient exercer prioritairement leurs activités dans les amphithéâtres, les salles de cours et les laboratoires de recherche. Il rappelle que leur statut particulier fixe un plafond de vingt enseignants universitaires pouvant exercer des responsabilités administratives, tout en affirmant que, dans les faits, plusieurs postes stratégiques du ministère sont aujourd'hui occupés par des universitaires. « L'ancien directeur des ressources humaines, le secrétaire général, le ministre lui-même ainsi que le directeur des affaires juridiques sont tous des enseignants universitaires, et la liste est encore longue », affirme-t-il. Au-delà de cette question, la CDT renouvelle sa demande de recrutement de nouveaux fonctionnaires afin de réduire la pression qui s'exerce sur les personnels des établissements universitaires et des cités universitaires. Faute de dialogue avec le ministère, le syndicat affirme ne plus disposer que d'un seul levier. « Le ministre continue d'éviter toute rencontre pour débattre de ces dossiers et rechercher des solutions. Il ne nous reste donc que le recours à des formes de mobilisation légitimes », conclut Abdelhak Hissan. The post Statut particulier, hausse salariale : la CDT accuse El Midaoui de bloquer le dialogue social appeared first on Hespress Français - Actualités du Maroc .

items.title: Ukrainian drones hit more sites of Russian online retailer Wildberries
items.description: Ukrainian drones have hit warehouses belonging to Russia’s biggest online retailer again, injuring 15 people, according to the company’s founder and regional governors. Logistics hubs run by Wildberries in two south-west cities were impacted by overnight attacks and workers evacuated, the founder said. It is the second time Ukraine has attacked the firm’s facilities in four days, with warehouses in Tambov and Elektrostal hit over the weekend, killing eight people. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said on Wednesday that Ukraine had hit logistics hubs in the Krasnodar and Stavropol regions “involved in providing the Russian army with drone components, navigation equipment and other gear”. Wildberries founder Tatyana Kim said workers were evacuated after the attacks on logistics centres in Krasnodar and Nevinnomyssk in the Stavropol region. “There are no words to describe all the feelings and emotions caused by the attacks on ordinary people, on our employees, on us, who are simply doing our jobs,” she wrote on Telegram. “But these situations demonstrate the most important thing: we must continue to do what we do for the benefit of people.” Five people were hurt in a warehouse complex on the outskirts of Nevinnomyssk, Governor Vladimir Vladimirov said, with everyone receiving outpatient treatment. In Krasnodar, ten people were injured in an attack on a warehouse complex, with three in a serious condition, the region’s governor Veniamin Kondratyev said. BBC Verify has analysed footage and confirmed fires in the two locations. Reports of the strikes first emerged just after 00:00 GMT, with the Russian state news agency TASS reporting that air defences had been activated in Nevinnomyssk. Dramatic footage filmed by locals began to emerge shortly after. In both locations, the impact appears substantial, with large fires engulfing the buildings and huge columns of smoke rising into the air. Krasnodar’s governor said the region was hit by a “massive attack last night”, with drone debris also hitting the grounds of a company in Armavir and killing one employee. The city administration reported that an oil depot was struck, causing a fire. Drone fragments also hit two apartment buildings in Krasnodar and two homes in the village of Dinskaya, Kondratyev said. There were no injuries. Zelensky said Ukraine had struck an oil depot, as well as a tanker and four cargo ships of the Russian shadow fleet in the waters of the Sea of Azov and Black Sea. Russia has been operating a “shadow fleet” to evade international sanctions imposed on its oil exports after its full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022. The overnight strikes mark the second targeting of Wildberries’ facilities in less than a week. Recently published satellite imagery gives an indication of the damage caused to other Wildberries sites that were hit by Ukrainian strikes over the weekend. In Elektrostal, a city near the capital Moscow, three buildings were completely gutted. Serhii Kuzan, chairman of the Ukrainian Security and Cooperation Centre, previously told the BBC that Wildberries’ website had been used by Russian volunteers to buy military equipment. “The primary rationale for striking Wildberries’ warehouses is to disrupt Russian logistics and the supply of dual-use goods, critical electronics, sanctioned goods and the like to the Russian army and Russian arms manufacturers,” he said. “The collateral damage from such strikes could also have a serious impact on the Russian economy, as well as a psychological effect on Russian society and, likely, on continued support for the war.” Unlike retail giant Amazon, Wildberries and its rival Ozon act almost entirely as marketplaces, connecting independent sellers with buyers across Russia, storing and delivering their goods, and handling payments. The recent strikes have impacted hundreds of sellers whose livelihoods depend on online marketplaces. Trader Elena, not her real name, told BBC Russian she had lost clothing she sells in the attacks, which is her only income. She said that “if there are more strikes, I probably won’t manage. It’s terrifying to keep loading my goods into what feels like a furnace”.

items.title: Court adjourns Shinkafi’s suit against APC, INEC to July 28
items.description: By Idris Salisu, Gusau The Federal High Court in Gusau, Zamfara State, on Tuesday adjourned until July 28 the suit filed by Dr. Sani Abdullahi Shinkafi challenging his disqualification from contesting the All Progressives Congress (APC) senatorial ticket for Zamfara North. The presiding judge, Justice Dikko Hassan, fixed the new date after hearing arguments on affidavits, counter-affidavits and preliminary objections filed by both the plaintiff and the defendants. Shinkafi is challenging the APC, the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) and Senator Sahabi Ya’u over his exclusion from the party’s primary election, which produced Senator Ya’u as the APC candidate for the Zamfara North Senatorial District. The proceedings were conducted virtually, as the presiding judge did not sit physically in court. Shinkafi, who contested in the APC primary held on May 19, is asking the court to determine the legality of his disqualification. He alleged that the primary election was fraught with irregularities and failed to comply with the Electoral Act 2022 (as amended), INEC guidelines and the APC’s electoral guidelines. Speaking with journalists after the proceedings, counsel to the plaintiff, Bello Galadi, said his client remained dissatisfied with the conduct of the primary election, alleging that the process did not comply with the party’s guidelines and that the results were manipulated. Galadi said one of the pending applications before the court sought an extension of time to respond to the counter-affidavits filed by INEC and Senator Sahabi Ya’u. He disclosed that he filed four motions before the court, all of which were considered. However, the fourth application, seeking leave to appeal and a stay of execution, was contested and adjourned for hearing. Counsel to the defendants, Salauddeen, urged the court to award N2 million against the plaintiff, alleging that the suit was being deliberately delayed. The court, however, declined the request and ruled that the plaintiff should be given time to file responses to the counter-affidavits submitted by the defendants. The post Court adjourns Shinkafi’s suit against APC, INEC to July 28 appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: ECG announces planned power outages in five regions
items.description: The Electricity Company of Ghana (ECG) has announced a series of planned maintenance exercises across the Tema, Ashanti, Accra West, Central, and Eastern regions for Wednesday, July 22, as part of ongoing efforts to improve service delivery, while engineers in the Accra West and Tema regions work to resolve network faults. In the Tema Region, planned maintenance will affect Sydal Farms, Community 24, Japan City, Teteman, Global, Addo Kope, Kasseh, Big Ada, Ada Foah, Totope, Treasure Island, Aqua Safari, and surrounding areas on Wednesday from 10:00 am to 4:00 pm. Meanwhile, customers in the Tema Region continue to experience unplanned outages due to a network fault. Affected areas include TT Brothers, Zonda Trucks, Macbaron Charlets, PFC, European Market, Movelle, Asaswa, Kaas, Afko Fisheries, Ocean Fare, We Two Sea, Fishing Harbour, Main Harbour, GPHA Headquarters, FDA, Black Star Line Building, Agrona, Dry Dock, Sahara, Nyame Na Onim, GAFCo, Buade, Iron Bar, GCB Training School, Adogonno, Ravico, Watch Tower, Junction Mall, Alton Hotel, Regimanuel Estates, Queensland International School, and surrounding areas. In the Ashanti Region, multiple outages are scheduled for Wednesday. Affected areas include Tabre, Sepaase, Maakro, Besease, Topman farms, Abrafo Kokoben, Koforidua, Ntensere, Manhyia, Abuakwa, Akyeremade, Old Tafo, New Road Junction, Bonwire, Amanpe, Adanwomase, Baman, Kumaca, Asokore Mampong, Royal Foam Factory, CCC, Asokore Mampong Municipal, Mamponteng, Dwumanafo, Aboaso, Hemang, Ankaase Hospital, Mpobi, Ejuratia, Kona, Asonomaso, parts of Agona, Kwapia, Wiaso, Watchman, Emena, Boadi, Apemso, Appiadu, Donyina, Deduako, Master Nkwanta, Mensoso, Grumesa, Nkransa, Mprekyere, Nsuta, Akutreso, Brekete, Abu, Ackwa-Atwereboana, and surrounding areas In the Accra West Region, planned maintenance will affect Mama’s Inn, Sahara, Bishop Deli School, King Solomon, NIC, Agape, St. Clare, Police Training School, Tesano, and surrounding areas on Wednesday from 9:00 am to 5:00 pm. Customers in the Accra West Region are also experiencing unplanned outages due to a transformer fault at Gbawe Top Base. Affected areas include Lesson Villa, Bodoo, Ophelia Nyantechi, Shawamah, High Tension, and surrounding areas. In the Central Region, planned maintenance will affect Bontroase, Ojobi, Senya township, Bonsuoku, Anopasu, Breku, Fetteh township, and surrounding areas from 9:00 am to 4:00 pm. In the Eastern Region, planned maintenance will affect Asuokaw, Mepom, Asikasu, Owurakesim, and surrounding areas from 10:00 am to 2:00 pm, while Old Estate, Paradise, Country Home, Veterinary, Kenke Factory, New Lands Hospital, Daddy’s Spot, and surrounding areas will be affected from 8:00 am to 5:00 pm. ECG has apologised to customers for the inconvenience and urged those in affected areas to make alternative arrangements and remain patient as the scheduled works are carried out.

items.title: Mouhoun/Formation : 241 encadreurs de l’Immersion patriotique obligatoire renforcent leurs capacités à Dédougou
items.description: BURKINA-MOUHOUN-DEDOUGOU-PATRIOTISME-IMMERSION-FORMATION Mouhoun/Formation : 241 encadreurs de l'Immersion patriotique obligatoire renforcent leurs capacités à Dédougou Dédougou, 21 juil. 2026 (AIB)-Le gouverneur de la région de Bankui, Babo Pierre Bassinga, a lancé, ce mardi 21 juillet 2026 à Dédougou, la formation des encadreurs et animateurs de l'Immersion patriotique obligatoire (IPO). Prévue pour durer trois jours, cette session vise à renforcer les capacités de 241 encadreurs qui assureront l'accompagnement de 4 152 nouveaux bacheliers sur les 11 sites d'immersion de la région, à compter du 27 juillet 2026. Organisée simultanément dans les autres chefs-lieux de province des régions de Bankui et du Sourou, cette formation constitue la dernière étape préparatoire avant le démarrage de la deuxième édition de l’Immersion patriotique obligatoire. Elle fait suite à la session nationale de formation des formateurs, tenue du 14 au 16 juillet 2026 à Ouagadougou, au cours de laquelle une soixantaine de cadres de la région ont été outillés sur le contenu pédagogique et les modalités de mise en œuvre de l’initiative. Le directeur régional de l’enseignement secondaire, de la formation professionnelle et technique de Bankui et Sourou, Raoul Sanon, a salué la mobilisation des acteurs et souligné que cette session permettra de restituer aux 241 encadreurs, animateurs et personnels des Forces de défense et de sécurité les acquis de la formation nationale de Ouagadougou. Il a précisé que 64 participants prennent part à la session de Dédougou et s’est dit convaincu que les enseignements tirés de l’édition 2025 contribueront à renforcer l’organisation et la qualité de l’Immersion patriotique obligatoire. Le gouverneur de la région de Bankui, Babo Pierre Bassinga, a exhorté les formateurs à assurer une restitution fidèle des acquis de la session nationale tout en estimant que la qualité de cette formation déterminera le succès de la deuxième édition de l’Immersion patriotique obligatoire. Tirant les leçons de l’édition 2025, il a indiqué que le dispositif organisationnel et les contenus pédagogiques ont été renforcés afin d’offrir une formation plus efficace. Le gouverneur a invité les encadreurs à transmettre aux appelés, avec professionnalisme et exemplarité, les valeurs de discipline, de civisme, de patriotisme et d’intégrité, en droite ligne de la vision des plus hautes autorités de former une jeunesse responsable et engagée au service de la Nation. L’inspecteur de l’enseignement secondaire et formateur, Hilaire Sanon, a indiqué que le programme comprend douze modules répartis entre les volets civil et militaire. Les enseignements civiques portent notamment sur le réveil africain, les luttes de résistance des peuples africains, les mouvements de renaissance du continent, ainsi que les fondements du patriotisme et de la souveraineté. Les modules assurés par les Forces de défense et de sécurité (FDS) concernent principalement l’ordre serré, la discipline et les notions élémentaires de formation militaire. Dans les régions de Bankui et du Sourou, 4 152 nouveaux bacheliers effectueront leur Immersion patriotique obligatoire sur onze sites, dont quatre à Dédougou, trois à Boromo et un respectivement à Nouna, Solenzo, Toma et Tougan. Prévue du 27 juillet au 26 août 2026, cette deuxième édition ambitionne de consolider les valeurs civiques, patriotiques et citoyennes auprès des jeunes admis au baccalauréat. Agence d’information du Burkina SB/HB/OO

items.title: Contractors on $500m World Bank road projects will face sanctions for poor work – Agbodza
items.description: Roads and Highways Minister Kwame Governs Agbodza has assured Parliament that road projects to be funded under the newly approved US$500 million World Bank credit facility will be subjected to strict monitoring to ensure quality execution. Speaking in Parliament on Tuesday, July 21, the Minister warned that contractors who fail to deliver work that meets the required standards or honour their contractual obligations would face sanctions, stressing that government would not tolerate poor workmanship on projects financed with public funds. “We are not going to compromise on quality. These projects will be closely monitored, and any contractor who fails to deliver according to the contract specifications will face the necessary sanctions,” Mr Agbodza said. He explained that the funding will support the rehabilitation of feeder roads and improve farm-to-market connectivity in selected districts, noting that the Ministry is determined to ensure value for money through effective supervision. “This investment is intended to improve access for farming communities and boost economic activity. We will ensure that every cedi and every dollar invested translates into durable roads that serve the people,” he added.

items.title: Guardiola se deja tentar por Italia para dirigir su selección
items.description: Pep Guardiola se ha convertido en el sueño del fútbol italiano para guiar su selección, y él de momento no ha dicho que no, se ha dejado tentar y se lo está pensando. Era un rumor desde hace meses, pero solo esta semana ha adquirido visos de realidad porque al menos ya habido una oferta formal, y lo relevante es que el exentrenador del City no la ha rechazado de entrada. Porque lo cierto es que había dejado el club británico con un anuncio muy serio de que quería tomarse una temporada sabática y estar con su familia : “No hay planes para entrenar por un tiempo. De lo contrario, estaría aquí. Necesito dar un paso atrás”, dijo en mayo. La decisión se espera en breve, en los próximos días. Seguir leyendo

items.title: Three killed, 11 injured in head-on collision at Ajumako Kokoben
items.description: Three people have died, while 11 others sustained varying degrees of injuries following a head-on collision involving two vehicles at Ajumako Kokoben in the Central Region. The accident, which occurred on Monday, July 20, 2026, involved a Toyota Isuzu with registration number GE 1361-25 and a Toyota Voxy with registration number CN 478-26. The Ghana National Fire Service (GNFS) said it received a distress call at about 12:10 p.m., with firefighters arriving at the scene nine minutes later. According to the Service, four victims were trapped in the wreckage and were rescued by firefighters, while seven other injured persons had already been assisted by passersby before the emergency team arrived. The injured victims were handed over to medical personnel and transported to a health facility for treatment. Preliminary information gathered from a passenger at the scene indicated that the driver of the Toyota Voxy was allegedly overtaking another vehicle at high speed when poor visibility caused by dust led to the collision with the oncoming Toyota Isuzu. The GNFS, however, said the exact cause of the accident is yet to be established as investigations continue. Following the rescue operation, firefighters secured the scene, cleared the damaged vehicles and debris from the road, and restored the free flow of traffic before leaving the area at about 2:07 p.m.

items.title: Pakistan condemns Houthis’ threats to Saudi Arabia, commercial shipping in Red Sea
items.description: Pakistan on Wednesday “unequivocally” condemned the recent threats issued by Houthis against Saudi Arabia and commercial shipping in the Red Sea, the Foreign Office said. Islamabad’s statement comes after Yemen’s Houthi rebels , aligned with Iran, announced an immediate maritime embargo on Saudi Arabia, opening a new front in the Middle East conflict. The naval blockade, if enforced, could cut off access to Saudi Arabia’s Red Sea ports, which have been vital for oil exports as renewed US-Iran fighting limits passage through the Strait of Hormuz. It remains unclear how the Houthis would enforce such a blockade. “Threats against commercial shipping and trading with the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia are unacceptable and violate established principles of international law,” the FO said in a statement on Wednesday, noting the “continued threats” by Houthis. Islamabad said it was “particularly concerned by reports of threats directed against vessels engaged in lawful commercial trade” with Saudi Arabia. “Such actions threaten regional security, undermine freedom of navigation, challenge the rules-based maritime order and jeopardise uninterrupted flow of global commerce,” the FO highlighted. Furthermore, it declared that “any hostile act against Pakistani-flagged vessels or Pakistani maritime interests will be regarded as a grave threat to Pakistan’s national security and sovereign interests”. The FO warned that Pakistan reserved its right to “take all necessary measures, including lawful use of force, in the exercise of self-defence to protect its maritime assets and national interests”, consistent with the United Nations Charter and international law. Pakistan also said it was “deeply concerned over attempts to drag” Saudi Arabia into the Middle East conflict, as fighting continues between the United States and Iran, which in turn has targeted American military sites in some Gulf countries. Islamabad reiterated its “unwavering support for the security, sovereignty, territorial integrity and prosperity of the brotherly Kingdom of Saudi Arabia”. “While remaining steadfast in its commitment to dialogue, diplomacy and regional de-escalation, Pakistan calls upon all parties to respect international law and ensure the safety and security of international maritime shipping,” the FO statement affirmed. Houthis’ Monday announcement came after the group and Saudi Arabia traded fire last week for the first time in years, threatening a 2022 truce that has held despite expiring. After Houthi fired missiles at Saudi Arabia on July 13, Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif had expressed Pakistan’s strong condemnation of the “blatant attacks” against the kingdom, without further specifying the attacks. Islamabad had also backed Riyadh at an emergency UN Security Council briefing on Yemen, calling for all parties concerned to resolve differences through dialogue and diplomacy. Under a “Strategic Mutual Defence Agreement” signed between the two in September 2025, Pakistan and Saudi Arabia are to treat any attack on either nation as an act of aggression against both. Pakistan and Saudi Arabia have maintained close defence and security ties for decades, and their cooperation has remained an important element of Islamabad’s regional diplomacy. Pakistan had invoked the defence pact through diplomatic intervention in early March to help deter heavier Iranian strikes against Saudi Arabia. The Bab al-Mandeb threat While it remains unclear how the Houthis would enforce such a blockade, their threats have raised concerns about maritime security and energy shipping in the Red Sea. On Tuesday, three oil tankers loaded with Saudi crude for China and India made U-turns in the Red Sea, heading towards the Suez Canal rather than ‌braving the Yemeni coast at the sea’s mouth, Reuters reported . The Houthis have previously tried to cut off shipping through the Red Sea, leveraging the Bab al-Mandeb strait , a chok­epoint at its southern tip which vessels must navigate when travelling to or from the Suez Canal and the Mediterranean. Saudi Arabia’s Red Sea port of Yanbu has been crucial for exporting oil, bypassing Iran’s effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz during its war with the United States. Crude oil exports from Saudi Arabia fell for a third straight month in May to a record low, data ​from the Joint Organisations Data Initiative (JODI) showed on Tuesday, with the ‌US-Iran conflict disrupting shipments across the Gulf. Exports fell to about 3.434 million barrels per day in May, from 3.986m bpd in April. US President Donald Trump said on Tuesday that his country would “ take care of ” the Houthis if they followed through on the threat to blockade Saudi Arabia’s ports.

items.title: Un agent des services spéciaux ukrainiens avec une bombe arrêté dans le métro de Saint-Pétersbourg (VIDÉO)
items.description: Le Centre des relations publiques du FSB a annonce, le 22 juillet, l'interpellation d'un citoyen russe de 21 ans a l'entree du metro de Saint-Petersbourg. Le jeune homme collaborait avec les services speciaux ukrainiens et preparait un attentat contre un employe d'une entreprise regionale du complexe militaro-industriel russe. L'arrestation, menee conjointement par le FSB et le ministere russe de l'Interieur, est intervenue lors d'un controle de securite a l'entree de la station de metro « Gorkovskaïa ». Un engin explosif artisanal camoufle en traceur GPS a ete decouvert dans le sac a dos du suspect, qui a immediatement ete arrete par les forces de l'ordre. Une video de l'operation, diffusee par le FSB, montre un employe du metro demander au passager de soumettre son sac au controle. Le passage du bagage au scanner a alors permis de detecter l'engin explosif. L'intervention des services de securite a ainsi permis de dejouer l'attentat avant le passage a l'acte. Des renseignements transmis sur le secteur de la defense Selon les elements communiques par le FSB, le suspect, ne en 2005, a pris contact avec un representant des services speciaux ukrainiens via Telegram. Il a ensuite execute plusieurs missions confiees par son contact ukrainien. Le jeune homme etait notamment charge de surveiller des employes d'entreprises du complexe militaro-industriel situees a Saint-Petersbourg et dans la region de Leningrad. Le FSB indique qu'il transmettait egalement des informations obtenues sur son lieu de travail, ainsi que des donnees cartographiques et geodesiques. Dans le cadre de la preparation de l'attentat, il a recu les coordonnees d'une cache ou se trouvait l'engin explosif artisanal. Apres avoir recupere le dispositif, il devait le placer sous le vehicule d'un employe d'une entreprise regionale du secteur de la defense. Il transportait encore l'engin dans son sac lorsqu'il a ete intercepte a l'entree du metro. Une motivation financiere et une enquete en cours Lors de son interrogatoire, le suspect a declare avoir agi dans l'espoir d'obtenir une remuneration. Selon le FSB, les fonds lui ont ete verses par son contact ukrainien sur un portefeuille de cryptomonnaies. Une enquete penale pour « assistance a une activite terroriste » a ete ouverte en vertu de la troisieme partie de l'article 205.1 du Code penal russe. Le tribunal a ordonne le placement du suspect en detention provisoire. Les enqueteurs cherchent desormais a identifier les autres personnes impliquees dans la transmission de renseignements et dans la preparation de l'attentat. Les investigations doivent permettre de reconstituer l'ensemble du reseau ayant participe a ces activites de sabotage et de terrorisme.

items.title: Kourittenga : La délégation spéciale adopte le cahier des charges d’un forage pastoral et d’un parc de vaccination
items.description: BURKINA-KOURITTENGA-SESSION-EXTRAORDINAIRE Kourittenga : La délégation spéciale adopte le cahier des charges d'un forage pastoral et d'un parc de vaccination Gounghin, 21 juil. 2026 (AIB)- La délégation spéciale de la commune de Gounghin a tenu, ce mardi 21 juillet 2026, sa deuxième session extraordinaire de l'année. Les membres ont examiné et adopté le cahier des charges relatif à la réalisation d'un forage pastoral à exhaure solaire et d'un parc de vaccination mixte à Gandéongo-Bogdin, dans le cadre de la mise en œuvre du projet REDD+. L’ouverture officielle de cette session extraordinaire a été faite par le Président de la délégation spéciale (PDS) de la commune de Gounghin, François Janvier Lessogo, en présence des responsables des différents services techniques, des membres du projet REED+ et ceux de la délégation spéciale. La session a permis l’examen et l’adoption du cahier des charges élaboré dans le cadre du projet REED+ pour la réalisation d’un forage pastoral à exhaure solaire et d’un parc à vaccination mixte à Gandéongo-Bogdin dans la commune de Gounghin. La présentation du cahier des charges par l’assistant-consultant dans le cadre du projet et par ailleurs juriste-expert foncier, Moussa Sanga, a servi de cadre pour vérifier la conformité du cahier des charges par rapport aux textes et d’échanger entre autres sur le statut du cahier des charges, sur l’organe qui va s’en charger de la gestion des deux infrastructures et sur le mode de règlement des conflits. Le premier Vice-président de la délégation spéciale de la commune de Gounghin, Paling-Wendé Pierre Epiphane Kuiré, s’est dit satisfait des échanges et les différents apports. « Nous demandons à la population à plus d’engagement et d’implication dans la gestion de ces ouvrages afin qu’ils puissent durés dans le temps et servir dans le cadre de notre Révolution progressiste populaire », a-t-il laissé entendre. Agence d’information du Burkina AWS/HB/OO

Le bénéfice net part du groupe recule toutefois de 39,7%, en raison d'un effet de base lié à un produit exceptionnel comptabilisé au premier semestre 2025, tandis que le groupe confirme ses perspectives pour l'ensemble de l'exercice The post Maroc Telecom : le chiffre d'affaires progresse de 5,4% au premier semestre 2026 appeared first on Médias24 - Numéro un de l'information économique marocaine .

items.title: Sud de la RDC: la société civile pointe la présence persistante de militaires sur des sites miniers illégaux
items.description: Le commandant de la 22e région militaire basée dans le Katanga a annoncé le retrait de soldats d’un site minier, situé non loin de Kolwezi, suite à l’instruction donnée le 10 juillet par le président de la RDC sur ces installations illégales. Mais, sur le terrain, des sources locales affirment qu’aucun soldat n’a quitté les lieux. Depuis 2019, plusieurs décisions ont été prises par les autorités congolaises pour retirer les forces armées des périmètres miniers, sans résultat effectif. Les militaires sont souvent accusés non seulement de participer aux activités d'extraction illégale mais aussi de violation des droits humains.
